meanings.definitionCn: 加入十字军， 投身正义运动

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + 向/朝/在 + 强壮/价值 + able

etymology.story: 1540s, "ineffectual," from un- (1) "not" + available.

morphology.mnemonic: rise high and roll forcefully + on

etymology.story: 1858, from seismo- + -ic..

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 相似/聚集

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French resembler "belike" (12c., Modern French ressemble), from re-, intensive prefix, + sembler "to appear, to seem, be like," from Latin simulare "to copy" (see similar (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: （日月周围的）晕轮, 光环, 荣光

etymology.story: 1560s, "ring of light around the sun or moon," from Latin halo (nominative halos), from Greek halos "disk of the sun or moon; ring of light around the sun or moon" (also "disk of a shield"); ""threshing floor; garden," of unknown origin.

morphology.mnemonic: 破裂 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin convectionem (nominative convectio) "the act of carrying," noun of action from past participle stem of convehere "to carry together," from com- "together" (see com-) + vehere "to carry" (see vehicle).

meanings.definitionCn: 破坏；失事；残骸；失去健康的人
（人或动物的）尸体；残骸；（除脏去头备食用的）畜体

etymology.story: late 13c., from Anglo-French carcois, from or influenced by Old French charcois (Modern French carcasse) "trunk of a body, chest, carcass," and Anglo-Latin carcosium "dead body," all of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 处女的；纯洁的；未经利用的，处于原始状态的

etymology.story: late 14c., "to cast down," from Old French aflicter, from Latin afflictare "to damage, harass, torment," frequentative of affligere (past participle afflictus) "to dash down, overthrow," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + fligere (past participle flictus) "to strike," from PIE root *bhlig- "to strike" (source also of Greek phlibein "to press, crush," Czech blizna "scar," Welsh blif "catapult").

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 拉紧/约束

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin constrictus, past participle of constringere "compress" (see constrain).
1580s, probably from gloom (n.) even though that word is not attested as early as this one.
1630s, from Latin fluctuatus, past participle of fluctuare "to undulate" (see fluctuation).

meanings.definitionCn: 关税， 进口税； 海关

etymology.story: 1560s, "to compute, to estimate by mathematical means," from Latin calculatus, past participle of calculare "to reckon, compute," from calculus (see calculus).
"give formal approval to," late 14c., autorisen, from Old French autoriser "authorize, give authority to" (12c.), from Medieval Latin auctorizare, from auctor (see author (n.)).

early 14c., concelen, from Old French conceler "to hide, conceal, dissimulate," from Latin concelare "to hide," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + celare "to hide," from PIE root *kel- (2) "to cover, conceal" (see cell).

meanings.definitionCn: 叙事的，叙述的；叙事体的

etymology.story: "a tale, story," 1560s, from Middle French narrative and from narrative (adj.)..

late 14c., "to hold, occupy, reside in" (without regard to ownership), a back formation from possession and in part from Old French possesser "to have and hold, take, be in possession of" (mid-13c.), from Latin possess-, past participle stem of possidere "to have and hold, possess, be master of, own," from posse "to be able," from potis "able, powerful" (see potent) + esse "to be" (see be).

meanings.definitionCn: 同时发生；巧合；一致；相符；

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 落入/巧合

etymology.story: 1705, "be identical in substance or nature," but from 1640s as a verb in English in Latin form, "occupy the same space, agree in position," from Medieval Latin coincidere (used in astrology), literally "to fall upon together," from Latin com- "together" (see co-) + incidere "to fall upon" (in- "upon" + cadere "to fall;" see case (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 中立的，中性的；中立国的；非彩色的
中立国；中立者；非彩色；齿轮的空档

etymology.story: mid-15c., "one who remains neutral," from Latin neutralis "of neuter gender," (see neutral (adj.)).

morphology.mnemonic: ring环，圆 + 与…有关的/像…的

meanings.definitionCn: 设计;发明;图谋;作出(计划);想出(办法)

etymology.story: early 13c., "to form, fashion;" c.

meanings.definitionCn: 表面上的,貌似真实的；显然的,明明白白的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French aparant "evident, obvious, visible," from Latin apparentem (nominative apparens) "visible, manifest," present participle of apparere (see appear).

late 14c., from Old English mearg "marrow," earlier mærh, from Proto-Germanic *mazga- (source also of Old Norse mergr, Old Saxon marg, Old Frisian merg, Middle Dutch march, Dutch merg, Old High German marg, German Mark "marrow"), from PIE *mozgo- "marrow" (source also of Sanskrit majjan-, Avestan mazga- "marrow," Old Church Slavonic mozgu, Lithuanian smagenes "brain").

meanings.definitionCn: 慢性的， 延续很长的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French triple or directly from Latin triplus (see triple (adj.)).

root.meaning: small group of people working within a larger organization

morphology.mnemonic: small group of people working within a larger organization + ule + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1753, with reference to cellular tissue, from Modern Latin cellularis "of little cells," from cellula "little cell," diminutive of cella (see cell).

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + something used for igniting + 使变成/变得

etymology.story: 1630s, "illuminated;" 1660s in the sense "well-informed;" past participle adjective from enlighten..
1748, "not producing or incapable of producing the desired effect," from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + efficient.

late 13c., from Old French statut, estatut "(royal) promulgation, (legal) statute," from Late Latin statutum "a law, decree," noun use of neuter past participle of Latin statuere "enact, establish," from status "condition, position" (see status)..

1670s, a back-formation from placation or else from Latin placatus "soothed, quiet, gentle, calm, peaceful," past participle of placare "to calm, appease, quiet, soothe, assuage," related to placere "to please" (see please).

meanings.definitionCn: 心脏的；心脏病的；贲门的
谦逊的；简陋的；（级别或地位）低下的；不大的
使谦恭；轻松打败（尤指强大的对手）；低声下气

etymology.story: late 14c., "render oneself humble" (intrans.), also "to bend, kneel or bow;" late 15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 能接近的； 易达到的
日用品；商品；农/矿产品；有用之物

etymology.story: early 15c., "benefit, profit, welfare;" later "a convenient or useful product," from Middle French commodité "benefit, profit," from Latin commoditatem (nominative commoditas) "fitness, adaptation, convenience, advantage," from commodus "suitable, convenient" (see commode).

morphology.mnemonic: 站立/设置 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1787, from statistics + -al (1).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + ic + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "verify, establish the credibility of," 1650s, from Medieval Latin authenticatus, past participle of authenticare, from authenticus (see authentic).

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 向下/移除/取消 + 悬挂/称量/支付 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1610s, "not dependent on something else," from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + dependent.

Old English clif "rock, promontory, steep slope," from Proto-Germanic *kliban (source also of Old Saxon clif, Old Norse klif, Middle Dutch klippe, Dutch klip, Old High German klep, German Klippe "cliff, promontory, steep rock").

meanings.definitionCn: 承认；容许；(比赛结束前)认输；退让

etymology.story: 1630s, from Middle French concéder or directly from Latin concedere "give way, yield, go away, depart, retire," figuratively "agree, consent, give precedence," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + cedere "to go, grant, give way" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 无双的， 无比的， 空前的

etymology.story: 1590s, from un- (1) "not" + past participle of parallel (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 貌似可信的，花言巧语的；貌似真实的，貌似有理的

etymology.story: 1540s, "acceptable, agreeable," from Latin plausibilis "deserving applause, acceptable," from plaus-, past participle stem of plaudere "to applaud" (see plaudit).

meanings.definitionCn: 发泄；报仇；造成（巨大的破坏或伤害）

etymology.story: Old English wrecan "avenge," originally "to drive, drive out, punish" (class V strong verb; past tense wræc, past participle wrecen), from Proto-Germanic *wrekan (source also of Old Saxon wrekan, Old Norse reka, Old Frisian wreka, Middle Dutch wreken "to drive, push, compel, pursue, throw," Old High German rehhan, German rächen "to avenge," Gothic wrikan "to persecute"), from PIE root *wreg- "to push, shove, drive, track down" (see urge (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 蒸发,挥发;沉淀;发射;消失

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + vapor + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "draw off (humors or spirits) as vapor," from Late Latin evaporatum, past participle of evaporare "disperse in vapor" (see evaporation).

meanings.definitionCn: 亮度， 发光度； 光明

morphology.mnemonic: 光 + 具有…性质的 + ity

etymology.story: 1630s, "quality of being luminous," from French luminosité or else a native formation from luminous + -ity.

mid-14c., from Old French fervent "fervent, ardent" (12c.), from Latin ferventem (nominative fervens) "boiling, hot, glowing," figuratively "violent, impetuous, furious," present participle of fervere "to boil, glow," from PIE root *bhreuə- "to boil, bubble" (see brew (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 委员会；佣金；犯；委任；委任状

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 发送 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "talkative and disrespectfully smart," see flippant..

late 14c., "pertaining to reason;" mid-15c., "endowed with reason," from Old French racionel and directly from Latin rationalis "of or belonging to reason, reasonable," from ratio (genitive rationis) "reckoning, calculation, reason" (see ratio)..
1630s, from French rescinder "cut off, cancel" (15c.), and directly from Latin rescindere "to cut off, tear off, abolish," from re- "back" (see re-) + scindere "to cut, split" (see shed (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 再继续,重新开始;再用;恢复

etymology.story: also résumé, 1804, "a summary," from French résumé, noun use of past participle of Middle French resumer "to sum up," from Latin resumere (see resume (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: to supervise for publication + …者/…器 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1741, "pertaining to an editor;" see editor + -al (2).
1764, from sediment + -ary.

name of a group of sugars (in commercial use, "sugar-syrup from starch"), 1840, from French glucose (1838), said to have been coined by French professor Eugène Melchior Péligot (1811-1890) from Greek gleukos "must, sweet wine," related to glykys "sweet" (see gluco-).

"edible substance in a nut or the stone of a fruit," Old English cyrnel "seed, kernel, pip," from Proto-Germanic *kurnilo- (source also of Middle High German kornel "a grain," Middle Dutch cornel "coarse meal"), from the root of corn "seed, grain" (see corn (n.1)) + -el, diminutive suffix.
1887 (transitive), 1934 (intransitive), from Greek metabole "a change" (see metabolism) + -ize.
1590s, from Latin excavatus, past participle of excavare "to hollow out," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + cavare "to hollow, hollow out," from cavus "cave" (see cave (n.)).

"lame," in Old English lemphalt "limping," from Proto-Germanic *haltaz (source also of Old Saxon, Old Frisian halt, Old Norse haltr, Old High German halz, Gothic halts "lame"), from PIE *keld-, from root *kel- "to strike, cut," with derivatives meaning "something broken or cut off" (source also of Russian koldyka "lame," Greek kolobos "broken, curtailed").

meanings.definitionCn: 微妙的；精细的；敏感的；狡猾的；稀薄的

morphology.mnemonic: 看见 + 具有…性质的 + ity

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French viscosite (13c.) or directly from Medieval Latin viscositatem (nominative viscositas), from Late Latin viscosus (see viscous)..

meanings.definitionCn: 成分；组件；[电子] 元件

etymology.story: 1640s, "constitutional element" (earlier "one of a group of persons," 1560s), from Latin componentem (nominative componens), present participle of componere "to put together" (see composite).

meanings.definitionCn: 淹没；压倒；受打击；覆盖；压垮

etymology.story: early 14c., "to turn upside down, to overthrow," from over- + Middle English whelmen "to turn upside down" (see whelm).

meanings.definitionCn: 公平的，公正的；不偏不倚的

morphology.mnemonic: in + to separate the hair + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "not partial, not favoring one over another," 1590s, from assimilated form of in- (1) "not, opposite of" + partial.

meanings.definitionCn: 会诊医师,顾问医生;顾问

etymology.story: 1690s, of persons going to oracles, from consult + -ant.

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + 头 + itis

etymology.story: "inflammation of the brain," 1843, from encephalo- "the brain" + -itis "inflammation."

meanings.definitionCn: 空前的, 前所未有的

root.meaning: thing or person that goes before another

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + thing or person that goes before another

etymology.story: 1620s, from un- (1) "not" + precedented.

meanings.definitionCn: 身高，身材；（精神、道德等的）高度

etymology.story: early 14c., "natural height of a body, height," from Old French stature, estature "build, structure," from Latin statura "height, size of body, size, growth," from PIE *ste-tu-, from root *stā- "to stand," with derivatives meaning "place or thing that is standing" (see stet).

meanings.definitionCn: 说明；例证，插图；举例说明

morphology.mnemonic: not well, poorly + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "fictitious narrative," 1560s, from Italian novella "short story," originally "new story," from Latin novella "new things" (source of Middle French novelle, French nouvelle), neuter plural or fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 突出， 显著； 卓越； 重要

etymology.story: 1590s, "projection," from obsolete French prominence (16c.), from Latin prominentia "a jutting out" (see prominent).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + in + 钱包/偿还

etymology.story: 1610s, from re- "back" + imburse "to pay, enrich," literally "put in a purse" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 难达到的；难接近的；难见到的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French inaccessible (14c.), from Late Latin inaccessibilis "unapproachable," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + accessibilis "approachable" (see accessible).

1560s, "pertaining to marriage," from Latin genialis "pleasant, festive," literally "pertaining to marriage rites," from genius "guardian spirit" (see genius), with here perhaps a special sense of "tutelary deity of a married couple." Originally used in English in the Latin literal sense; meaning "cheerful, friendly" first recorded 1746.

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 太阳/独自

meanings.definitionCn: 陆地包围的, 内陆水域的

etymology.story: also land-locked, "almost shut in by land," 1620s, from land (n.) + past participle of lock (v.)..

1560s, "to stand in front of," from Middle French confronter (15c.), from Medieval Latin confrontare "assign limits, adjoin," from Latin com- "together" (see com-) + frontem (nominative frons) "forehead" (see front (n.)).
1650s, back-formation from obesity and in part from Latin obesus "fat, stout, plump," past participle of obedere "that has eaten itself fat" (see obesity).
1610s, from Latin dominatus, past participle of dominari "to rule, dominate, to govern," from dominus (see domain).

morphology.mnemonic: 模仿/复制 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1530s, a back-formation from imitation or imitator, or else from Latin imitatus, past participle of imitari "to copy, portray."

meanings.definitionCn: 计算；估算；用计算机计算

etymology.story: 1630s, from French computer, from Latin computare "to count, sum up, reckon together," from com- "with" (see com-) + putare "to reckon," originally "to prune" (see pave).

meanings.definitionCn: 生殖;翻版;繁殖;复制,仿造

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 向前/支持 + 引导

etymology.story: 1610s, "to produce again," from re- "again" + produce (v.), probably on model of French reproduire (16c.).

meanings.definitionCn: 骨架，骨骼；纲要；骨瘦如柴的人
骨骼的；骨瘦如柴的；概略的

etymology.story: 1570s, from Modern Latin sceleton "bones, bony framework of the body," from Greek skeleton soma "dried-up body, mummy, skeleton," from neuter of skeletos "dried-up" (also, as a noun, "dried body, mummy"), from skellein "dry up, make dry, parch," from PIE root *skele- "to parch, wither" (see sclero-).

meanings.definitionCn: 制度；建立；（社会或宗教等）公共机构；习俗

morphology.mnemonic: in + 建立/放置 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1550s, "bundle of straw to lie on," possibly from or related to Low German or obsolete Flemish pad "sole of the foot," which is perhaps from PIE *pent- "to tread, go" (see find (v.)), but see path (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 文盲的；不识字的；没受教育的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 石 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "uneducated, unable to read and write" (originally meaning Latin), from Latin illiteratus "unlearned, unlettered, ignorant; without culture, inelegant," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + literatus "educated," literally "furnished with letters" (see literate).

late 15c., from Middle French délinquant, from present participle of délinquer (15c.), and directly from Latin delinquentum (nominative delinquens), present participle of delinquere "to fail; be wanting, fall short; offend," from de- "completely" (see de-) + linquere "to leave" (see relinquish).

meanings.definitionCn: 交叉；十字路口；交集；交叉点

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + cut切割 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1550s, "act or fact of crossing," from Middle French intersection (14c.) and directly from Latin intersectionem (nominative intersectio) "a cutting asunder, intersection," noun of action from past participle stem of intersecare "intersect, cut asunder," from inter- "between" (see inter-) + secare "to cut" (see section (n.)).
late 14c., from Latin dispersus, past participle of dispergere "to scatter," from dis- "apart, in every direction" (see dis-) + spargere "to scatter" (see sparse).

meanings.definitionCn: 好奇的， 好问的； 爱打听的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French inquisitif, from Late Latin inquisitivus "making inquiry," from Latin inquisit-, past participle stem of inquirere "seek after, search for; examine, investigate" (see inquire).

meanings.definitionCn: 古怪的，反复无常的，不稳定的

etymology.story: late 14c., "wandering, moving," from Old French erratique "wandering, vagrant" (13c.) and directly from Latin erraticus "wandering, straying, roving," from erratum "an error, mistake, fault," past participle of errare "to wander; to err" (see err).
"by, for," mid-13c., from Old French par, per, from Latin per (see per)..

meanings.definitionCn: 焚化， 焚毁， 把…烧成灰烬

etymology.story: "burn to ashes" (transitive), 1550s, from Medieval Latin incineratus, past participle of incinerare "reduce to ashes," from in- "into" (see in- (2)) + a verb from Latin cinis (genitive cineris) "ashes," from PIE root *keni- "dust, ashes" (source also of Greek konis "dust").
1610s, from well (adv.) + gerundive of be..

meanings.definitionCn: 恶毒的；恶意的；堕落的；有错误的；品性不端的；剧烈的

etymology.story: late 14c., "unwholesome, impure, of the nature of vice, wicked, corrupting, pernicious, harmful;" of a text, "erroneous, corrupt," from Anglo-French vicious, Old French vicios "wicked, cunning, underhand; defective, illegal" (Modern French vicieux), from Latin vitiosus (Medieval Latin vicious) "faulty, full of faults, defective, corrupt; wicked, depraved," from vitium "fault" (see vice (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 侵蚀， 腐蚀； 削弱， 损害

etymology.story: 1610s, "gnaw or eat away" (transitive), a back-formation from erosion, or else from French éroder, from Latin erodere "to gnaw away, consume," from assimilated form of ex- "away" (see ex-) + rodere "gnaw" (see rodent).
"aircraft pilot," 1887, from French aviateur, from Latin avis (see aviary) + -ateur.

meanings.definitionCn: 庞大的，巨大的；凶暴的，极恶的

etymology.story: 1530s, "abnormal" (usually in a bad sense), from Latin enormis "out of rule, irregular, shapeless; extraordinary, very large," from assimilated form of ex- "out of" (see ex-) + norma "rule, norm" (see norm), with English -ous substituted for Latin -is.

morphology.mnemonic: like new wine, + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French moistour "moisture, dampness, wetness" (13c., Modern French moiteur), from moiste (see moist)..
"of, pertaining to, or obtained from sulfur," also sulphuric, 1790, from French sulfurique; see sulfur + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 怠惰，懒惰；[脊椎] 树懒

etymology.story: late 12c., "indolence, sluggishness," formed from Middle English slou, slowe (see slow (adj.)) + abstract formative -th (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 心智健全的,神志清醒的,明智的,稳健的

etymology.story: 1721, back-formation from sanity or else from Latin sanus "sound, healthy," in figurative or transferred use, "of sound mind, rational, sane," also, of style, "correct;" of uncertain origin.
mid-15c., from Latin frustratus, past participle of frustrari "to deceive, disappoint, make vain," from frustra (adv.) "in vain, in error," related to fraus "injury, harm" (see fraud).

meanings.definitionCn: 外部的；表面的；[药] 外用的；外国的；外面的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + tern + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French externe or directly from Latin externus "outside, outward" (from exterus; see exterior) + -al (1).

1727, from Latin sparsus "scattered," past participle of spargere "to scatter, spread," from PIE root *(s)preg- (2) "to jerk, scatter" (source also of Sanskrit parjanya- "rain, rain god," Avestan fra-sparega "branch, twig," literally "that which is jerked off a tree," Old Norse freknur "freckles," Swedish dialectal sprygg "brisk, active," Lithuanian sprogti "shoot, bud," Old Irish arg "a drop").

early 13c., "conditions surrounding and accompanying an event," from Old French circonstance "circumstance, situation," also literally, "outskirts" (13c., Modern French circonstance), from Latin circumstantia "surrounding condition," neuter plural of circumstans (genitive circumstantis), present participle of circumstare "stand around, surround, encompass, occupy, take possession of" from circum "around" (see circum-) + stare "to stand" from PIE root *stā- "to stand" (see stet).

meanings.definitionCn: 务实的； 实事求是的； 实用主义观点的

etymology.story: 1610s, "meddlesome, impertinently busy," short for earlier pragmatical, or else from Middle French pragmatique (15c.), from Latin pragmaticus "skilled in business or law," from Greek pragmatikos "fit for business, active, business-like; systematic," from pragma (genitive pragmatos) "a deed, act; that which has been done; a thing, matter, affair," especially an important one; also a euphemism for something bad or disgraceful; in plural, "circumstances, affairs" (public or private), often in a bad sense, "trouble," literally "a thing done," from stem of prassein/prattein "to do, act, perform" (see practical).

root.meaning: clamping tool with two jaws closed by a screw

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + clamping tool with two jaws closed by a screw

etymology.story: 1560s, "to look at again," from Middle French reviser (13c.), from Latin revisere "look at again, visit again, look back on," frequentative of revidere (past participle revisus), from re- "again" (see re-) + videre "to see" (see vision).

early 13c., autorite "book or quotation that settles an argument," from Old French auctorité "authority, prestige, right, permission, dignity, gravity; the Scriptures" (12c.; Modern French autorité), from Latin auctoritatem (nominative auctoritas) "invention, advice, opinion, influence, command," from auctor "master, leader, author" (see author (n.)).
"inflammable colorless and odorless gas, marsh gas," 1867, coined from chemical suffix -ane + syllable abstracted from methyl..

1530s, from Anglo-French paramont, Old French paramont "above" (in place, order, degree), mid-14c., from Old French par "by," from Latin per "through, for, by" (see per (prep.)) + amont "up," from a mont "upward" (see amount (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 经度的， 经线的； 纵的； 长度的

morphology.mnemonic: to grow long, lengthen, + 状态/品质 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1706, from Latin longitudo (see longitude) + -al (1)..
"a body of lawmakers," 1670s; see legislator + -ure..
1640s, from Medieval Latin deleterius, from Greek deleterios "noxious," from deleter "destroyer," from deleisthai "to hurt, injure."

meanings.definitionCn: 神经的；神经系统的；背的；神经中枢的

morphology.mnemonic: 神经 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "pertaining to a nerve or nerves," 1830, from Greek neuron "nerve" (see neuro-) + adjectival suffix -al (1).

morphology.mnemonic: semi + 共同/联合 + 引导 + …者/…器

etymology.story: 1838, "material whose electrical conductivity is between that of a conductor and that of an insulator," from semi- + conductor.
"with condition that," early 15c., conjunction use of past participle of provide.
1580s, "one allowed to live in a house rented by another" (usually for a consideration), from in (adj.) "inside" + mate (n.) "companion." OED suggests the first element is perhaps originally inn.

meanings.definitionCn: 仪器；工具；乐器；手段；器械

etymology.story: late 13c., "musical instrument, mechanical apparatus for producing musical sounds," from Old French instrument, enstrument "means, device; musical instrument" (14c., earlier estrument, 13c.) and directly from Latin instrumentum "a tool, an implement; means, furtherance; apparatus, furniture; ornament, dress, embellishment; a commission, authorization; a document," from instruere "arrange, furnish" (see instruct (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 分配/免除 + able

etymology.story: 1530s, "not subject to dispensation," from Medieval Latin *indispensabilis, from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + dispensabilis, from Latin dispensare "disburse, administer, distribute (by weight)" (see dispense).

morphology.mnemonic: to place before + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "organic compound produced in animal bodies to regulate activity and behavior," 1905, from Greek hormon "that which sets in motion," present participle of horman "impel, urge on," from horme "onset, impulse," from PIE *or-sma-, from root *er- (1) "to move, set in motion." Used by Hippocrates to denote a vital principle; modern scientific meaning coined by English physiologist Ernest Henry Starling (1866-1927).

meanings.definitionCn: 致命的；重大的；毁灭性的；命中注定的

etymology.story: late 14c., "decreed by fate," also "fraught with fate," from Middle French fatal (14c.) and directly from Latin fatalis "ordained by fate, decreed, destined; destructive, deadly," from fatum (see fate (n.)); sense of "causing or attended with death" in English is from early 15c.

root.meaning: street noted for clubs, bars, etc.

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/外部 + street noted for clubs, bars, etc.

etymology.story: 1570s, "to pass in running," from out + Middle English strip "move quickly," of unknown origin.
1650s, "elliptic in shape;" see elliptic + -al (1).

morphology.mnemonic: 战争 + …者/…器 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1927, in psychology, from behavior + -al (1)..

meanings.definitionCn: 摔跤； 全力对付； 用力移动

etymology.story: Old English *wræstlian, frequentative of wræstan "to wrest" (see wrest) with -el (3).

meanings.definitionCn: 促动；激励，鼓励，作为…的动机

etymology.story: 1863, "to stimulate toward action," from motive + -ate (2); perhaps modeled on French motiver or German motivieren.

meanings.definitionCn: 投射；规划；突出；发射；推测

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 投/掷 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 15c., in alchemy, "transmutation by casting a powder on molten metal; 1550s in the cartographical sense "drawing of a map or chart according to scale," from Middle French projection, from Latin proiectionem (nominative proiectio), from past participle stem of proicere (see project (n.)).

Old English hrycg "back of a man or beast," probably reinforced by Old Norse hryggr "back, ridge," from Proto-Germanic *khrugjaz (source also of Old Frisian hregg, Old Saxon hruggi, Dutch rug, Old High German hrukki, German Rücken "the back"), of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 乘；使增加；使繁殖；使相乘

etymology.story: mid-12c., multeplier, "to cause to become many," from Old French multiplier, mouteplier (12c.) "increase, get bigger; flourish; breed; extend, enrich," from Latin multiplicare "to increase," from multiplex (genitive multiplicis) "having many folds, many times as great in number," from comb.

late 14c., of animals, "to graze;" early 15c., of humans, "to lead to pasture, to feed by putting in a pasture," from Old French pasturer (12c., Modern French pâturer, from pasture (see pasture (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + 起源/种类

etymology.story: early 14c., "beget, procreate," from Old French engendrer (12c.) "give birth to, beget, bear; cause, bring about," from Latin ingenerare "to implant, engender, produce," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + generare "beget, create" (see generation).
1738, from hypothesis + -ize.

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1680s, from approve + -al (2).
1640s, "involving many parts," from French multiple (14c.), from Late Latin multiplus "manifold," from Latin multi- "many, much" (see multi-) + -plus "-fold" (see -plus).

early 15c., from Old French lucratif "profitable" and directly from Latin lucrativus "gainful, profitable," from lucratus, past participle of lucrari "to gain," from lucrum "gain, profit" (see lucre).

1560s, "a figure, drawn or painted," a back formation from portraiture or directly from Middle French portrait, from Old French portret (13c.), noun use of past participle of portraire "to paint, depict" (see portray).

meanings.definitionCn: 磁铁；[电磁] 磁体；磁石
集中；浓缩；全神贯注；聚集

root.meaning: to rest as at a center

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + to rest as at a center + 使…/具有…的

meanings.definitionCn: 通用的;多才多艺的,多方面的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 爆发/鼓掌 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1620s, "action of driving out with violence and noise," from French explosion, from Latin explosionem (nominative explosio) "a driving off by clapping," noun of action from past participle stem of explodere "drive out by clapping" (see explode for origin and sense evolution).

meanings.definitionCn: 酸度, 酸性, [医]酸过多, 胃酸过多

etymology.story: 1610s, from French acidité (26c.) or directly from Latin aciditatem (nominative aciditas), noun of quality from Latin acidus (see acid (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 牧师的；牧人的；田园生活的；乡村的

morphology.mnemonic: 和平 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "of or pertaining to shepherds," early 15c., from Old French pastoral (13c.), from Latin pastoralis "of herdsmen, of shepherds," from pastor (see pastor (n.)).
late 14c., "lamentation, grief," from Old French complainte (12c.) "complaint, lament," noun use of fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 使自动化, 自动操作

etymology.story: "to convert to automatic operation," 1954, back-formation from automated (q.v.).
1837, from French personnel (a contrastive term to matériel), noun use of personnel (adj.) "personal," from Old French personel (see personal)..
mid-15c., "quality of being neither good nor bad, neutral quality," from Latin indifferentia "want of difference, similarity," noun of quality from indifferentem (see indifferent).

1530s, from Middle French porcelaine and directly from Italian porcellana "porcelain" (13c.), literally "cowrie shell," the chinaware so called from resemblance of its lustrous transparency to the shiny surface of the shells.

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 终止/界限 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1540s, "drive away," from Latin exterminatus, past participle of exterminare "drive out, expel, put aside, drive beyond boundaries," also, in Late Latin "destroy," from phrase ex termine "beyond the boundary," from ex- "out of" (see ex-) + termine, ablative of termen "boundary, limit, end" (see terminus).

meanings.definitionCn: 含义；暗示；牵连，卷入；可能的结果，影响

morphology.mnemonic: in + 折叠/交织 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "action of entangling," from Latin implicationem (nominative implicatio) "an interweaving, an entanglement," noun of state from past participle stem of implicare "involve, entangle; embrace; connect closely, associate," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + plicare "to fold" (see ply (v.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 凶猛的,残忍的;狂热的,强烈的

etymology.story: mid-13c., "proud, noble, bold, haughty," from Old French fers, fiers, nominative form of fer, fier "strong, overwhelming, violent, fierce, wild; proud, mighty, great, impressive" (Modern French fier "proud, haughty"), from Latin ferus "wild, untamed, uncultivated; waste, desert;" figuratively "wild, uncultivated, savage, cruel," from PIE root *ghwer- "wild; wild animal" (source also of Greek ther, Old Church Slavonic zveri, Lithuanian zveris "wild beast").

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + to swear profanely + …者/…器

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French precurseur and directly from Latin praecursor "forerunner," agent noun from past participle stem of praecurrere, from prae "before" (see pre-) + currere "to run" (see current (adj.)).

root.meaning: self-imposed restraint on freedom of words or actions; habit of keeping back the feelings

morphology.mnemonic: self-imposed restraint on freedom of words or actions; habit of keeping back the feelings + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "act of reserving," from Old French reservation (14c.) and directly from Late Latin reservationem (nominative reservatio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin reservare (see reserve (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 成批的，大块的；交通堵塞的

etymology.story: "obstruct," 1590s, from French bloquer "to block, stop up," from Old French bloc (see block (n.)).
early 15c., "return to consciousness; restore to health," from Middle French revivre (10c.), from Latin revivere "to live again," from re- "again" (see re-) + vivere "to live" (see vital).
1971, from micro- + computer..

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 移动 + 状态/行为/过程 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1869, "relating to promotion or advancement," from promotion + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 干旱的；不毛的，[农] 荒芜的

etymology.story: 1650s, "dry, parched," from French aride (15c.) or directly from Latin aridus "dry, arid, parched," from arere "to be dry," from PIE root *as- "to burn, glow" (see ash (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 下流的；说话粗鄙恶劣的；无礼的

etymology.story: "using such language as only the licence of a buffoon can warrant" [Johnson], 1570s, from scurrile "coarsely joking" (c.

late 14c., fibre "a lobe of the liver," also "entrails," from Medieval Latin fibre, from Latin fibra "a fiber, filament; entrails," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps related to Latin filum "a thread, string" (see file (n.1)) or from root of findere "to split" (see fissure).

late 14c., "to go on," also "to emanate from, result from," from Old French proceder (13c., Modern French procéder) and directly from Latin procedere (past participle processus) "go before, go forward, advance, make progress; come forward," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + cedere "to go" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 市镇自治机关；法人；公司，企业

morphology.mnemonic: body体（corpor也作corp） + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., "persons united in a body for some purpose," from such use in Anglo-Latin, from Late Latin corporationem (nominative corporatio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin corporare "to embody" (see corporate).

meanings.definitionCn: 解散；解雇；开除；让离开；不予理会、不予考虑

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin dimissus, past participle of dimittere "send away, send different ways; break up, discharge; renounce, abandon," from dis- "apart, away" (see dis-) + mittere "send, let go" (see mission).

meanings.definitionCn: 潜在的；根本的；在下面的；优先的
放在…的下面；为…的基础；优先于（underlie的ing形式）

etymology.story: 1610s, present participle adjective from underlie..

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 声音 + ance

etymology.story: mid-15c., in acoustics, "prolongation of sound by reverberation;" 1660s, "act of resonating;" from Middle French resonance (15c.), from Latin resonantia "echo," from resonare "to sound again" (see resound).
early 15c., from Late Latin turbulentia "trouble, disquiet," from Latin turbulentus (see turbulent).

meanings.definitionCn: 智力,聪明;理解力;情报,消息,报导

etymology.story: late 14c., "the highest faculty of the mind, capacity for comprehending general truths;" c.

morphology.mnemonic: 变换 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of changing," from Old French mutacion (13c.), and directly from Latin mutationem (nominative mutatio) "a changing, alteration, a turn for the worse," noun of action from past participle stem of mutare "to change" (see mutable).

"pale yellow, brownish yellow," Old English fealu "reddish yellow, yellowish-brown, tawny, dusk-colored" (of flame, birds' feet, a horse, withered grass or leaves, waters, roads), from Proto-Germanic *falwa- (source also of Old Saxon falu, Old Norse fölr, Middle Dutch valu, Dutch vaal, Old High German falo, German falb), from PIE *pal-wo- "dark-colored, gray" (source also of Old Church Slavonic plavu, Lithuanian palvas "sallow;" Greek polios "gray" (of hair, wolves, waves), Sanskrit palitah, Welsh llwyd "gray;" Latin pallere "to be pale"), suffixed form of root *pel- (2) "pale" (see pallor).

also instal, formerly also enstall, early 15c., "place in (ecclesiastical) office by seating in an official stall," from Old French installer (14c.) or directly from Medieval Latin installare, from Latin in- "in" (see in- (2)) + Medieval Latin stallum "stall," from a Germanic source (compare Old High German stal "standing place;" see stall (n.1)).

morphology.mnemonic: untouched, + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1610s, "act of bringing together the parts of a whole," from French intégration and directly from Late Latin integrationem (nominative integratio) "renewal, restoration," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin integrare "make whole," also "renew, begin again" (see integrate).

early 15c., "a sign, that which indicates," from Latin indicationem (nominative indicatio) "an indicating; valuation," noun of action from past participle stem of indicare "point out, show," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + dicare "proclaim" (see diction)..

meanings.definitionCn: 可预知的， 能预测的

etymology.story: 1804, from foresee + -able.
1732, "like a microscope;" see microscope + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 体积，容量；大多数，大部分；大块
使扩大，使形成大量；使显得重要
[医]接种疫苗, 种痘, 牛痘疤

morphology.mnemonic: 空 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1800, used by British physician Edward Jenner (1749-1823) for the technique he devised of preventing smallpox by injecting people with the cowpox virus (variolae vaccinae), from vaccine (adj.) "pertaining to cows, from cows" (1798), from Latin vaccinus "from cows," from vacca "cow" (Latin bos "cow" being originally "ox," "a loan word from a rural dialect" according to Buck, who cites Umbrian bue).
1850, keramic, from Greek keramikos, from keramos "potter's clay, pottery, tiles," perhaps from a pre-Hellenic word.

meanings.definitionCn: 环行,周线,巡回;电路,线路

morphology.mnemonic: 地方 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1640s, formed in English from Latin loco "from a place" (ablative of locus "place") + motionem (nominative motio) "motion, a moving.".

meanings.definitionCn: 内脏；肠子；剧情；胆量；海峡；勇气；直觉；肠
取出内脏；摧毁（建筑物等）的内部
简单的；本质的，根本的；本能的，直觉的

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 拉紧/约束

etymology.story: 1530s, from Latin restrictus, past participle of restringere (see restriction).

morphology.mnemonic: 命令/委托 + 使…/具有…的 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1570s, "of the nature of a mandate," from Late Latin mandatorius "pertaining to a mandator," from Latin mandatus, past participle of mandare (see mandate (n.)).

mid-15c., catyrpel, probably altered (by association with Middle English piller "plunderer;" see pillage) from Old North French caterpilose "caterpillar" (Old French chatepelose), literally "shaggy cat" (probably in reference to the "wooly-bear" variety), from Late Latin catta pilosa, from catta "cat" (see cat (n.)) + pilosus "hairy, shaggy, covered with hair," from pilus "hair" (see pile (n.3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 普及， 流行， 盛行

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 强壮/价值 + ance

etymology.story: 1590s, "fact of having mastery," from Middle French prévalence (15c.), from Late Latin praevalentia, from praevalens, present participle of praevalere (see prevalent).

meanings.definitionCn: 结合；[语] 连接词；同时发生

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 连接 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., originally of planets, from Old French conjonction "union, joining, sexual intercourse" (12c.), from Latin coniunctionem (nominative coniunctio), from past participle stem of coniugare "join together" (see conjugal).

"a number of symptoms occurring together," 1540s, from medical Latin, from Greek syndrome "concurrence of symptoms, concourse of people," from syndromos "place where several roads meet," literally "a running together," from syn- "with" (see syn-) + dromos "a running, course" (see dromedary).
"to look closely," 1590s, variant of piren (late 14c.), with a long -i-, probably related to or from East Frisian piren "to look," of uncertain origin.

mid-15c., "to oppress for the holding of a belief or opinion," from Middle French persécuter "pursue, torment, open legal action" (14c.), from Latin persecutus, past participle of persequi "to pursue" (see persecution).

meanings.definitionCn: 必要的，不可避免的；紧急的；命令的，专横的；势在必行的；[语]祈使的
必要的事；命令；需要；规则；[语]祈使语气

morphology.mnemonic: the British Empire, + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., in grammar; later "something imperative" (c.

early 14c., "fact or right of succeeding someone by inheritance," from Old French succession "inheritance; a following on" (13c.), from Latin successionem (nominative successio) "a following after, a coming into another's place, result," noun of action from successus, past participle of succedere (see succeed).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: mid-14c., "in agreement with," from according + -ly (2).

late 14c., "inference, conclusion," from Old French consequence "result" (13c., Modern French conséquence), from Latin consequentia, from consequentem (nominative consequens), present participle of consequi "to follow after," from com- "with" (see com-) + sequi "to follow" (see sequel).

meanings.definitionCn: 强健的；健康的；粗野的；粗鲁的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Middle French robuste (14c.) and directly from Latin robustus "strong and hardy," literally "as strong as oak," originally "oaken," from robur, robus "hard timber, strength," also "a special kind of oak," named for its reddish heartwood, from Latin ruber "red" (related to robigo "rust"), from PIE *reudh- (see red (adj.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 线的，线型的；直线的，线状的；长度的

morphology.mnemonic: one's occupation, branch of business + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1640s, from French linéaire, from Latin linearis "belonging to a line," from linea "string, line" (see line (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: oc + 状态/行为/过程 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1850, from occupation + -al (1).

"to strip off," developed from Old English pilian "to peel, skin, decorticate, strip the skin or ring," and Old French pillier, both from Latin pilare "to strip of hair," from pilus "hair" (see pile (n.3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 开拓；回收再利用；改造某人，使某人悔改

etymology.story: early 14c., "call back a hawk to the glove," from Old French reclamer "to call upon, invoke; claim; seduce; to call back a hawk" (12c.) and directly from Latin reclamare "cry out against, contradict, protest, appeal," from re- "opposite, against" (see re-) + clamare "cry out" (see claim (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 共同/联合 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1640s, "not agreeing in substance or form;" 1650s, "self-contradictory," from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + consistent.

meanings.definitionCn: 公开指责,公然抨击;谴责

etymology.story: early 14c., "announce," from Old French denoncier (12c., Modern French dénoncer), from Latin denuntiare "to announce, proclaim; denounce, menace; command, order," from de- "down" + nuntiare "proclaim, announce," from nuntius "messenger" (see nuncio).

"edge, rim," mid-15c., from Old French verge "twig, branch; measuring rod; penis; rod or wand of office" (12c.), hence, from the last sense, "scope, territory dominated" (as in estre suz la verge de "be under the authority of"), from Latin virga "shoot, rod, stick, slender green branch," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 过敏的， 变态反应的， 变应性的

etymology.story: 1911, from allergy + -ic; perhaps modeled on French allergique (1906).
1640s, "structure for enclosing or supporting," from frame (n.) + work (n.).
"surroundings," 1877, from French milieu, "middle, medium, mean," literally "middle place" (12c.), from mi "middle" (from Latin medius; see medial (adj.)) + lieu "place" (see lieu)..
1844, in figurative sense, from pivot (n.) + -al (1)..

morphology.mnemonic: 微小 + musical instrument, + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: late 14c., "distress, oppression," from Old French constreinte "binding, constraint, compulsion" (Modern French contrainte), fem.

1590s, from Late Latin entitatem (nominative entitas), from ens (genitive entis) "a thing," proposed by Caesar as present participle of esse "be" (see is), to render Greek philosophical term to on "that which is" (from neuter of present participle of einai "to be;" see essence).

late 14c., "fill with disease, render pestilential; pollute, contaminate; to corrupt morally," from Latin infectus, past participle of inficere "to stain, tinge, dye," also "to corrupt, stain, spoil," literally "to put in to, dip into," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + facere "to make, do, perform" (see factitious).

1650s, from Late Latin depletionem (nominative depletio) "blood-letting," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin deplere "to empty," literally "to un-fill," from de- "off, away" (see de-) + plere "to fill" (see pleio-)..

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 拥有/保持

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin prohibitus, past participle of prohibere "to hold back, restrain" (see prohibition).

"economical in use," 1590s, from Middle French frugal, from Latin frugalis, from undeclined adjective frugi "useful, proper, worthy, honest; temperate, economical," originally dative of frux (plural fruges) "fruit, produce," figuratively "value, result, success," related to fructus (see fruit), from PIE *bhrug- "agricultural produce," also "to enjoy." Sense evolved in Latin from "useful" to "profitable" to "economical."
late 14c., "to make free," from Old French franchiss-, past participle stem of franchir "to free" (12c.), from franc "free" (see frank (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不可逆的；不能取消的；不能翻转的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 再/重新/回 + metrical composition + able

etymology.story: 1620s, of decrees, etc., "that cannot be overturned or undone," from assimilated form of in- (1) "not, opposite of" + reversible.

"to hold or exercise sovereign power," late 13c., from Old French regner "rule, reign" (12c.), from Latin regnare "have royal power, be king, rule, reign," from regnum "kingship, dominion, rule, realm," related to regere "to rule, to direct, keep straight, guide" (see regal).

late 14c., "pertaining to heaven," from Old French celestial "celestial, heavenly, sky-blue," from Latin caelestis "heavenly, pertaining to the sky," from caelum "heaven, sky; abode of the gods; climate," which is of uncertain origin; perhaps from PIE *kaid-slo-, perhaps from a root also found in Germanic and Baltic meaning "bright, clear" (compare Lithuanian skaidrus "shining, clear;" Old English hador, German heiter "clear, shining, cloudless," Old Norse heið "clear sky").
Old English foreseon "have a premonition," from fore- "before" + seon "to see, see ahead" (see see (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + socio + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "allied, connected, paired," from Latin associatus, past participle of associare (see associate (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 多顾虑的， 谨慎的； 一丝不苟的， 严谨的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Anglo-French scrupulus, Middle French scrupuleux, or directly from Latin scrupulosus, from scrupulus (see scruple).

morphology.mnemonic: 相同/识别 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s as a term in logic; general sense of "being the same or very similar" is from 1630s, from Medieval Latin identicus "the same," from Late Latin identitas "identity, sameness," ultimately from comb.
mid-13c., "make a plea in court," from Anglo-French pleder, Old French plaidier, "plead at court" (11c.), from Medieval Latin placitare, from Late Latin placitum (see plea).
early 14c., kidenere, a word of unknown origin, perhaps a compound of Old English cwið "womb" (see bowel) + ey "egg" (see egg (n.)) in reference to the shape of the organ.
1790, "procured from milk," in the chemical name lactic acid, which is so called because it was obtained from sour milk.

meanings.definitionCn: 落后；迟延；防护套；囚犯；桶板
落后于；押往监狱；加上外套

etymology.story: early 14c., "to gather by acquisition, scrape together," especially grains left in the field after harvesting, but the earliest use in English is figurative, from Old French glener "to glean" (14c., Modern French glaner) "to glean," from Late Latin glennare "make a collection," of unknown origin.

morphology.mnemonic: 自我 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1948, in the manufacturing sense, coined by Ford Motor Co.

meanings.definitionCn: 野心勃勃的；有雄心的；热望的；炫耀的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin ambitiosus "going around to canvass for office," from ambitio (see ambition).

root.meaning: to rest as at a center

morphology.mnemonic: to rest as at a center + 与…有关的/形容词 + ity

etymology.story: 1640s; see central (adj.) + -ity..
1590s, from Latin contemptus (see contempt).

meanings.definitionCn: 慈善；施舍；慈善团体；宽容；施舍物

etymology.story: mid-12c., "benevolence for the poor," from Old French charité "(Christian) charity, mercy, compassion; alms; charitable foundation" (12c., Old North French carité), from Latin caritatem (nominative caritas) "costliness, esteem, affection" (in Vulgate often used as translation of Greek agape "love" -- especially Christian love of fellow man -- perhaps to avoid the sexual suggestion of Latin amor), from carus "dear, valued," from PIE *karo-, from root *ka- "to like, desire" (see whore (n.)).
1580s, "relating to an academy," also "collegiate, scholarly," from Latin academicus "of the Academy," from academia (see academy).

meanings.definitionCn: 功能；[数] 函数；职责；盛大的集会

morphology.mnemonic: 站立/设置 + 状态/行为/过程 + ery

meanings.definitionCn: 股份，股票；库存；血统；树干；家畜

etymology.story: in reference to conversation, literature, "recurring, commonplace" (as in stock phrase), 1738, figurative use from sense "kept in store for constant use" (1620s), from stock (n.2)..

morphology.mnemonic: to place before + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French comparaison (12c.), from Latin comparationem (nominative comparatio), noun of action from past participle stem of comparare "make equal with, liken, bring together for a contest," literally "to couple together, to form in pairs," from com- "with" (see com-) + parare "prepare" (see pare)..

meanings.definitionCn: 仪式的；例行的；礼节性的
汹涌；大浪，波涛；汹涌澎湃；巨涌
分发， 分配； 提供

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French dispenser "give out" (13c.), from Latin dispensare "disburse, administer, distribute (by weight)," frequentative of dispendere "pay out," from dis- "out" (see dis-) + pendere "to pay, weigh" (see pendant).

meanings.definitionCn: 有害的；恶性的；致命的；险恶的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French pernicios (13c., Modern French pernicieux) and directly from Latin perniciosus "destructive," from pernicies "destruction, death, ruin," from per- "completely" (see per) + necis "violent death, murder," related to necare "to kill," nocere "to hurt, injure, harm," noxa "harm, injury" (see noxious).

morphology.mnemonic: slender, flexible slip of wood + er

meanings.definitionCn: 回复；重提；返祖遗传；归还
任意的；无差别的；不分皂白的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 不/分离 + 判定/控告 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "not carefully discriminating, done without making distinctions," 1640s, from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + discriminate (adj.)..

"plant-eating animal," 1851, from Modern Latin Herbivora (1830) or French herbivore (1748), from neuter plural of Latin herbivorus, from herba "a plant" (see herb) + vorare "devour, swallow" (see voracity)..

meanings.definitionCn: 使一致；使和解；调停，调解；使顺从

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 调和/和解

etymology.story: mid-14c., of persons, from Old French reconcilier (12c.) and directly from Latin reconcilare "to bring together again; regain; win over again, conciliate," from re- "again" (see re-) + concilare "make friendly" (see conciliate).

meanings.definitionCn: 决定；使适应；使健康；以…为条件
诱惑,引诱;吸引,使感兴趣

etymology.story: "to retail," 1837 in modern use, a colloquial back-formation from peddler.

meanings.definitionCn: 道德准则， 行为准则， 伦理标准；

etymology.story: late 14c., ethik "study of morals," from Old French etique "ethics, moral philosophy" (13c.), from Late Latin ethica, from Greek ethike philosophia "moral philosophy," fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 无法区分的， 难以分辨的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + to push, thrust + able

etymology.story: 1640s, "not clearly perceived;" 1650s, "incapable of being told apart," from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + distinguishable.

1540s, "to disown, disinherit (children)," from Latin abdicatus, past participle of abdicare "to disown, disavow, reject" (specifically abdicare magistratu "renounce office"), from ab- "away" (see ab-) + dicare "proclaim" (see diction).

morphology.mnemonic: meta + bol + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: in physiology sense, 1878, from French métabolisme, from Greek metabole "a change," from metaballein "to change," from meta- "over" (see meta-) + ballein "to throw" (see ballistics)..

meanings.definitionCn: 邪恶的；不幸的；有害的；讨厌的

etymology.story: Old English yfel (see evil (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 卡特尔；企业联合；垄断联盟；俘虏交换条约

etymology.story: 1550s, "a written challenge," from Middle French cartel (16c.), from Italian cartello "placard," diminutive of carta "card" (see card (n.1)).
"about to happen or appear," late 15c., present participle adjective from Middle English forthcomen, from Old English forðcuman "to come forth, come to pass;" see forth + come (v.).
1650s, "of one's own country," from French patriotique or directly from Late Latin patrioticus, from Greek patriotikos, from patriotes (see patriot).

meanings.definitionCn: 混乱, 断层, 脱臼

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + 地方 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

morphology.mnemonic: to make more complex + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French complication, from Latin complicationem (nominative complicatio), noun of action from past participle stem of complicare "to fold together, fold up, roll up," from com- "together" (see com-) + plicare "to fold, weave" (see ply (v.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 照射； 照耀， 照亮

morphology.mnemonic: in + 射线/放射 + 使…/具有…的

meanings.definitionCn: 指责， 谴责， 责难

morphology.mnemonic: 发光/闪耀 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French combat (16c.), from combattre (see combat (v.))..
"an act of levying," early 15c., from Anglo-French leve, Old French levée "a raising, lifting; levying," noun use of fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 满足；纵容；使高兴；使沉迷于…

etymology.story: formerly also endulge, 1630s, "to grant as a favor;" 1650s, "to treat with unearned favor" (in reference both to persons and desires), a back-formation from indulgence (q.v.), or else from Latin indulgere "be complaisant, be indulgent, yield; give oneself up to;" probably a compound verb with first element in- "in," but the second element is obscure.

late 13c., "to follow with hostile intent," from Anglo-French pursuer and directly from Old French poursuir (Modern  French poursuivre), variant of porsivre "to chase, pursue, follow; continue, carry on," from Vulgar Latin *prosequare, from Latin prosequi "follow, accompany, attend; follow after, escort; follow up, pursue," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + sequi "follow" (see sequel).

1530s, "reversal of what is expected" (especially a fatal turning point in a drama), from Latin catastropha, from Greek katastrophe "an overturning; a sudden end," from katastrephein "to overturn, turn down, trample on; to come to an end," from kata "down" (see cata-) + strephein "turn" (see strophe).

meanings.definitionCn: 运送， 搬运， 转运；表达； 传播

etymology.story: 1650s; see detriment + -al (1).
"bony framework of the head," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 可能,可能性;可能的事,希望

etymology.story: late 14c., "condition of being possible," from Old French possibilité (13c.) and directly from Latin possibilitatem (nom.

meanings.definitionCn: 小巷；[航][水运] 航线；车道；罚球区

etymology.story: Old English lane, lanu "narrow hedged-in road," common Germanic (cognates: Old Frisian lana, Middle Dutch lane, Dutch laan "lane, alley, avenue," Old Norse lön "small, oblong hayrick," in modern use "row of houses"), but of unknown origin.

early 15c., from Old French contaminer, from Latin contaminatus, past participle of contaminare "to defile," from contamen "contact, pollution," from com- "together" (see com-) + *tag-, base of tangere "to touch" (see tangent (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 暗指，转弯抹角地说到；略为提及，顺便提到

etymology.story: 1530s, "mock," from Middle French alluder or directly from Latin alludere "to play, sport, joke, jest," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + ludere "to play" (see ludicrous).

late 14c., "a rising, height of something, height to which something is elevated," from Old French elevation and directly from Latin elevationem (nominative elevatio) "a lifting up," noun of action from past participle stem of elevare "lift up, raise" (see elevate).

meanings.definitionCn: 再起， 复活， 再现

etymology.story: 1834; see resurgent + -ence..

meanings.definitionCn: 捕食性动物； 食肉动物； 奴役他人者； 剥削者， 掠夺者

etymology.story: 1862, from Latin praedator "plunderer," from praedari "to rob" (see predation).

meanings.definitionCn: 一致， 符合；协议， 条约

etymology.story: late 13c., accourd, from Old French acord "agreement," a back-formation from acorder (see accord (v.))..
also updraught, "rising air current," 1909, from up (adj.) + draft (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 自动的；无意识的；必然的

etymology.story: 1610s, from French consécutif (16c.), from Medieval Latin consecutivus, from Latin consecutus "following closely," past participle of consequi (see consequence).
"number of times one quantity is contained in another," mid-15c., from Latin quotiens "how often? how many times?; as often as," pronominal adverb of time, from quot "how many?" (see quote (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: adj. 右边的，右侧的 + ity

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French dexterité (16c.), from Latin dexteritatem (nominative dexteritas) "readiness, skillfulness, prosperity," from dexter "skillful," also "right (hand)" (source of Old French destre, Spanish diestro, etc.), from PIE root *deks- "on the right hand," hence "south" to one facing east (source also of Sanskrit daksinah "on the right hand, southern, skillful;" Avestan dashina- "on the right hand;" Greek dexios "on the right hand," also "fortunate, clever;" Old Irish dess "on the right hand, southern;" Welsh deheu; Gaulish Dexsiva, name of a goddess of fortune; Gothic taihswa; Lithuanian desinas; Old Church Slavonic desnu, Russian desnoj).
also merchandize, "to buy and sell; to market," late 14c.; see merchant + -ize.

meanings.definitionCn: 引导;管理;指挥(乐队);传导

etymology.story: early 15c., "to guide," from Latin conductus, past participle of conducere "to lead or bring together" (see conduce).

meanings.definitionCn: 天鹅绒，丝绒；天鹅绒似的东西

etymology.story: early 14c., probably from Old Provençal veluet, from Vulgar Latin *villutittus, diminutive of Vulgar Latin *villutus "velvet," literally "shaggy cloth," from Latin villus "shaggy hair, nap of cloth, tuft of hair," probably a dialectal variant of vellus "fleece," from PIE *wel-no-, suffixed form of root *wel- (4) "to tear, pull" (see svelte)..
1580s, "having quantity," from Medieval Latin quantitativus, from stem of Latin quantitas (see quantity).

meanings.definitionCn: 治理；套；驾驭；披上甲胄；利用
马具；甲胄；挽具状带子；降落伞背带；日常工作

1650s, from drain + -age..

meanings.definitionCn: 维护，维修；保持；生活费用

morphology.mnemonic: to carry on, keep up + ance

etymology.story: mid-14c., "bearing, deportment," from Old French maintenance "upkeep; shelter, protection,: from maintenir (see maintain).

meanings.definitionCn: 捕食；牺牲者；被捕食的动物
器械，器具，仪器；机构，组织

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin apparatus "tools, implements, equipment; preparation, a preparing," noun of state from past participle stem of apparare "prepare," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + parare "make ready" (see pare)..

suffix.meaning: thing or animal spoken about before.

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 脚/儿童 + thing or animal spoken about before. + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "military campaign; the act of rapidly setting forth," from Old French expedicion "an expediting, implementation; expedition, mission" (13c.) and directly from Latin expeditionem (nominative expeditio) "an enterprise against an enemy, a military campaign," noun of action from past participle stem of expedire "make ready, prepare" (see expedite).
"a nerve cell with appendages," 1891, from German Neuron, from Greek neuron (see neuro-).
"a vertebrate animal," 1826, from Latin vertebratus (Pliny), from vertebra "joint or articulation of the body, joint of the spine" (see vertebra).

meanings.definitionCn: 音乐会,演奏会;一齐,一致

etymology.story: 1660s, "agreement, accord, harmony," from French concert (16c.), from Italian concerto "concert, harmony," from concertare "bring into agreement," in Latin "to contend, contest, dispute," from com- "with" (see com-) + certare "to contend, strive," frequentative of certus, variant past participle of cernere "separate, decide" (see crisis).

meanings.definitionCn: 衰退，[核] 衰减；腐烂，腐朽
衰退，[核] 衰减；腐烂，腐朽
吸收；吸引；承受；理解；使…全神贯注

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French absorber (Old French assorbir, 13c.), from Latin absorbere "to swallow up," from ab- "from" (see ab-) + sorbere "suck in," from PIE root *srebh- "to suck, absorb" (source also of Armenian arbi "I drank," Greek rhopheo "to sup greedily up, gulp down," Lithuanian srebiu "to drink greedily").

meanings.definitionCn: 好信誉；有资格接受信用贷款

etymology.story: 1850, from re- "again" + vamp (v.) "patch up, replace the upper front part of a shoe." An earlier verb was new-vamp (1630s).

morphology.mnemonic: 打破 + 行为/过程/结果

meanings.definitionCn: 关系；关心；关心的事；忧虑

etymology.story: 1640s (transitive), "to brood upon, watch jealously" (figurative); 1721 in literal sense "to sit on (eggs) to hatch them," from Latin incubatus, past participle of incubare "to lie in or upon," also in the figurative sense "brood" (see incubation).

"small, ornamental human representation in pottery or other material work," 1854, from French figurine (16c.), from Italian figurina, diminutive of figura, from Latin figura "shape, form, figure" (see figure (n.)).

root.meaning: a contest or single proceeding in a public sport

morphology.mnemonic: a contest or single proceeding in a public sport + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s, "pertaining to events," from French éventuel, from Latin event-, stem of evenire (see event).

late 14c., from Old French refrain "chorus" (13c.), alteration of refrait, noun use of past participle of refraindre "repeat," also "break off," from Vulgar Latin *refrangere "break off," alteration of Latin refringere "break up, break open" (see refraction) by influence of frangere "to break." Influenced in French by cognate Provençal refranhar "singing of birds, refrain." The notion is of something that causes a song to "break off" then resume.

early 15c., from Old French pacte "agreement, treaty, compact" (14c.), from Latin pactum "agreement, contract, covenant," noun use of neuter past participle of pacisci "to covenant, to agree, make a treaty," from PIE root *pag- "fix, join together, unite, make firm" (source also of Sanskrit pasa- "cord, rope," Avestan pas- "to fetter," Greek pegnynai "to fix, make firm, fast or solid," Latin pangere "to fix, to fasten," Slavonic paž "wooden partition," Old English fegan "to join," fon "to catch seize")..

meanings.definitionCn: 有责任的，有义务的；应受罚的；有…倾向的；易…的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "bound or obliged by law," probably from Anglo-French *liable, from Old French lier "to bind, tie up, fasten, tether; bind by obligation," from Latin ligare "to bind, to tie" (see ligament).
1650s, "freight loaded on a ship," from Spanish cargo "burden," from cargar "to load, impose taxes," from Late Latin carricare "to load on a cart" (see charge (v.)).
"pertaining to Spain" (especially ancient Spain) 1580s, from Latin Hispanicus, from Hispania "Iberian Peninsula," from Hispanus "Spaniard" (see Spaniard).

Old English dag "dough," from Proto-Germanic *daigaz "something kneaded" (source also of Old Norse deig, Swedish deg, Middle Dutch deech, Dutch deeg, Old High German teic, German Teig, Gothic daigs "dough"), from PIE *dheigh- "to build, to form, to knead" (source also of Sanskrit dehah "body," literally "that which is formed," dih- "to besmear;" Greek teikhos "wall;" Latin fingere "to form, fashion," figura "a shape, form, figure;" Gothic deigan "to smear;" Old Irish digen "firm, solid," originally "kneaded into a compact mass").

meanings.definitionCn: 机密的；表示信任的；获信任的

morphology.mnemonic: to share a secret with + ance + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1759, from Latin confidentia (see confidence) + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 元素；要素；原理；成分；自然环境

etymology.story: late 14c., "not owing or payable; unjustly demanded," also "not appropriate, unseasonable," also "excessive," from un- (1) "not" + past participle of due (v.).

"universal remedy," 1540s, from Latin panacea, a herb (variously identified) that would heal all illnesses, from Greek panakeia "cure-all," from panakes "all-healing," from pan- "all" (see pan-) + akos "cure," from iasthai "to heal" (see -iatric).
1755, from sand (n.) + bar (n.1)..
late 14c., from Old French abundant and directly from Latin abundantem (nominative abundans) "overflowing," present participle of abundare "to overflow" (see abound).
late 14c., from Latin pigmentum "coloring matter, pigment, paint," figuratively "ornament," from stem of pingere "to color, paint" (see paint (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 在前/预先 + that which is cast

meanings.definitionCn: 谦恭有礼；有礼貌的举止

etymology.story: early 13c., curteisie, from Old French curteisie (Modern French courtoisie), from curteis "courteous" (see courteous).

1640s (as a past participle adjective, 1570s), from Late Latin deterioratus, past participle of deteriorare "get worse, make worse," from Latin deterior "worse, lower, inferior, meaner," contrastive of *deter "bad, lower," from PIE *de-tero-, from demonstrative stem *de- (see de).
1520s, originally a second crop of grass grown after the first had been harvested, from after + -math, a dialectal word, from Old English mæð "a mowing, cutting of grass" (see math (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 污染物质， 有害物质

morphology.mnemonic: make physically foul + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1888, from pollute + -ant.
"to cut in two," 1640s, from Modern Latin bisectus, from Latin bi- "two" (see bi-) + secare "to cut" (see section (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 引诱,劝使;引起,导致;感应

etymology.story: formerly also enduce, late 14c., "to lead by persuasions or other influences," from Latin inducere "lead into, bring in, introduce, conduct; persuade; suppose, imagine," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + ducere "to lead" (see duke (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 标准/规范 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1835, displaced older abnormous (1742) and rival anormal (1835) under influence of Latin abnormis "deviating from a rule," from ab- "off, away from" (see ab-) + norma "rule" (see norm).

meanings.definitionCn: 流出物， 废水， 污水

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin effluentem "flowing out" (see effluence).

meanings.definitionCn: 登记；使加入；把记入名册；使入伍
使用铅版；套用老套，使一成不变
[化学] 分子；微小颗粒，微粒

etymology.story: 1794, "extremely minute particle," from French molécule (1670s), from Modern Latin molecula, diminutive of Latin moles "mass, barrier" (see mole (3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 怀旧的，回忆往事的；耽于回想的

morphology.mnemonic: indulge in reminiscences + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1705, from Latin reminiscentem (nominative reminiscens), present participle of reminisci "call to mind, remember" (see reminiscence).

early 15c., from Latin memorandum "(thing) to be remembered," neuter singular of memorandus "worthy of remembrance, noteworthy," gerundive of memorare "to call to mind," from memor "mindful of" (see memory).

meanings.definitionCn: 辞去,辞职,使听从(于),使顺从

etymology.story: late 14c., "give up, surrender, abandon, submit; relinquish," from Old French resigner "renounce, relinquish" (13c.), from Latin resignare "to check off, annul, cancel, give back, give up," from re- "opposite" (see re-) + signare "to make an entry in an account book," literally "to mark" (see sign (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 复杂的；精致的；久经世故的；富有经验的
使变得世故；使迷惑；篡改（sophisticate的过去分词形式）

morphology.mnemonic: 智慧 + …主义者/…专家 + ic + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 14c., of alcoholic distillates, brandy (ardent spirits), etc., from Old French ardant (13c.) "burning, hot; zealous," from Latin ardentem (nominative ardens) "glowing, fiery, hot, ablaze," also used figuratively of passions, present participle of ardere "to burn," from PIE root *as- "to burn, glow" (source also of Old English æsce "ashes;" see ash (n.1)).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1570s, from Latin anomalia, from Greek anomalia "inequality," noun of quality from anomalos "uneven, irregular," from an-, privative prefix, "not" (see an- (1)) + homalos "even," from homos "same" (see same)..

meanings.definitionCn: 招待,款待;表演文娱节目

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 持有/保持 + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1530s, "provision for support of a retainer; manner of social behavior," now obsolete, along with other 16c.

late 15c., "to settle," from Middle French resider (15c.) and directly from Latin residere "sit down, settle; remain behind, rest, linger; be left," from re- "back, again" (see re-) + sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

meanings.definitionCn: 司法权， 审判权， 裁判权

morphology.mnemonic: 判断/法律 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 14c., jurisdiccioun, jurediction, etc., "administration of justice," from Old French juridicion (13c., Modern French juridiction) and directly from Latin iurisdictionem (nominative iurisdictio) "administration of justice, jurisdiction," from phrase iuris dictio, genitive of ius "law, right" (see jurist) + dictio "a saying" (see diction).

meanings.definitionCn: 并发的；一致的；同时发生的
[数] 共点；同时发生的事件

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French concurrent or directly from Latin concurrentem (nominative concurrens), present participle of concurrere (see concur).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + 拉/吸引 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 可利用性， 可得性； 利用的可能性； 可利用的人或物

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 强壮/价值 + able + ity

etymology.story: 1803, from available + -ity..

mid-15c., "that points out, states, or declares" (grammatical), from Old French indicatif (14c.), from Late Latin indicativus "serving to point out," from indicat-, past participle stem of Latin indicare "to point out, show" (see indication).

meanings.definitionCn: 详细审查；监视；细看；选票复查

etymology.story: early 15c., "a vote to choose someone to decide a question," from Late Latin scrutinium "a search, inquiry" (in Medieval Latin, "a mode of election by ballot"), from Latin scrutari "to examine, investigate, search," from PIE root *skreu- "to cut; cutting tool" (see shred (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不可剥夺的， 不可分割的

etymology.story: "that cannot be given up," 1640s, from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + alienable (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 促进,发扬;提升,提拔;增进,助长

etymology.story: late 14c., "to advance (someone) to a higher grade or office," from Old French promoter and directly from Latin promotus, past participle of promovere "move forward, advance; cause to advance, push onward; bring to light, reveal," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + movere "to move" (see move (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不兼容的， 不能和谐共存的， 不协调的， 合不来的

etymology.story: "that cannot coexist or be conjoined," mid-15c., from Medieval Latin incompatibilis, from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + compatibilis (see compatible).
1819, from French de luxe, literally "of luxury," from Latin luxus "excess, abundance" (see luxury)..

meanings.definitionCn: 装规矩, 假正经的行为

etymology.story: 1709, from prude + -ery and in part from French pruderie.

1590s, "arithmetical table," also "official list of customs duties on imports or exports; law regulating import duties," from Italian tariffa "tariff, price, assessment," Medieval Latin tarifa "list of prices, book of rates," from Arabic ta'rif "information, notification, a making known; inventory of fees to be paid," verbal noun from arafa "he made known, he taught." Sense of "classified list of charges made in a business" is recorded from 1757.
1650s, "condition of flowing," from Latin currens, present participle of currere "to run" (see current (adj.)); the sense of a flow or course extended 1699 (by John Locke) to "circulation of money.".
early 15c., "to deal out or apportion," from Latin distributus, past participle of distribuere "to divide, distribute" (see distribution).

meanings.definitionCn: 征募； 从军， 参军； 谋取

etymology.story: also inlist, 1690s (trans.), 1753 (intrans.), from en- (1) "make, put in" + list (n.).

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 价格/价值 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1767, "a lowering of value" (originally of currency), noun of action from depreciate.

meanings.definitionCn: 保留；扣留，滞留；记忆力；闭尿

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 伸展/趋向 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin retentionem (nominative retentio) "a retaining, a holding back," noun of action from past participle stem of retinere (see retain).

meanings.definitionCn: 法律(规)；立法，法律的制定(或通过)

morphology.mnemonic: 读/选择 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1650s, "the enacting of laws," from French législation (14c.), from Late Latin legislationem (nominative legislatio), properly two words, legis latio, "a proposing (literally 'bearing') of a law;" see legislator.
1847, "to question," quies, perhaps from Latin qui es? "who are you?," first question in oral exams in Latin in old-time grammar schools.

meanings.definitionCn: (in)参与,参加;分享,分担;含有,带有

etymology.story: 1530s, back-formation from participation, or else from Latin participatus, past participle of participare "to share, share in, participate in; to impart," from particeps "partaking, sharing," from parti, past participle of partir "to divide" (see part (n.)) + -cip-, weak form of stem of capere "to take" (see capable).

in early use also engredient, early 15c., "something forming part of a mixture," from Latin ingredientem (nominative ingrediens) "that which enters into" (a compound, recipe, etc.), present participle of ingredi "go in, enter," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + gradi "to step, go" (see grade (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 剩余；[贸易] 顺差；盈余；过剩

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French sorplus "remainder, extra" (12c., Modern French surplus), from Medieval Latin superplus "excess, surplus," from Latin super "over" (see super-) + plus "more" (see plus).

meanings.definitionCn: 使加固，使加强；(把)联为一体，合并

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 太阳/独自 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1510s, "to compact into one body," from Latin consolidatus, past participle of consolidare "to make solid," from com- "together" (see com-) + solidare "to make solid" (see solid).
"steep slope of a road or railroad," 1835, principally in American English, probably from grade (n.) by analogy of quotient, etc.

early 15c., "to forbid, prohibit," back-formation from inhibition or else from Latin inhibitus, past participle of inhibere "to hold in, hold back, keep back." Psychological sense (1876) is from earlier, softened meaning of "restrain, check, hinder" (1530s).

morphology.mnemonic: 对抗/撤回 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1820s, "act of taking back," also "retraction of a statement," from withdraw + -al (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 多产， 丰饶； 繁殖力， 生殖力

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin fecunditatem (nominative fecunditas) "fruitfulness, fertility," from fecundus "fruitful, fertile" (see fecund)..

meanings.definitionCn: 不同的；不相干的；全异的
挑衅的；目中无人的，蔑视的；挑战的

etymology.story: 1837, from French défiant, present participle of défier (see defy).

late 14c., "play or other serious literary work with an unhappy ending," from Old French tragedie (14c.), from Latin tragedia "a tragedy," from Greek tragodia "a dramatic poem or play in formal language and having an unhappy resolution," apparently literally "goat song," from tragos "goat" + oide "song" (see ode).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 弯曲 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1620s, from reflect + -ive.

morphology.mnemonic: 错误地/不当地 + to place before

etymology.story: 1640s, from mis- (1) + represent.
early 15c., from Gaelic clann "family, stock, offspring," akin to Old Irish cland "offspring, tribe," both from Latin planta "offshoot" (see plant (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 缩图；微型画；微型图画绘画术

etymology.story: "small," 1714, from minature (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 绘制…的图表；在海图上标出；详细计划；记录；记述；跟踪（进展或发展
内阁；橱柜；展览艺术品的小陈列室

etymology.story: 1540s, "secret storehouse, treasure chamber," from Middle French cabinet "small room" (16c.), diminutive of Old French cabane "cabin" (see cabin); perhaps influenced by (or rather, from) Italian gabbinetto, diminutive of gabbia, from Latin cavea "stall, stoop, cage, den for animals" (see cave (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 眼/视 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1560s, from optic + -al (1).

late 14c., "origin, source, beginning; rule of conduct; axiom, basic assumption; elemental aspect of a craft or discipline," from Anglo-French principle, Old French principe "origin, cause, principle," from Latin principium (plural principia) "a beginning, commencement, origin, first part," in plural "foundation, elements," from princeps (see prince).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + domin + …的人/处于…状态 + 以…方式/副词化
统治 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1797, from French antagonisme or directly from late Greek antagonisma, noun of action from antagonizesthai "to struggle against" (see antagonist)..
1630s (adj.); 1650s (n.), from French litigant, from Latin litigantem (nominative litigans), present participle of litigare (see litigation)..

meanings.definitionCn: 集团； 财团； 社团， 协会

etymology.story: 1829, from Latin consortium "fellowship, participation, society," from consors (genitive consortis; see consort (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 胚， 胚胎 <；事物的萌芽期

etymology.story: "fetus in utero at an early stage of development," mid-14c., from Medieval Latin embryo, properly embryon, from Greek embryon "a young one," in Homer, "young animal," later, "fruit of the womb," literally "that which grows," from assimilated form of en "in" (see en- (2)) + bryein "to swell, be full.".
"personal outfit or attire," early 14c., also "ship's rigging," from Old French apareil "preparation," from apareillier (see apparel (v.)).
"reckoning of numbers," late 14c., verbal noun from account (v.).
1932, from French télécommunication (see tele- + communication).

meanings.definitionCn: 开始，创始；发起；使初步了解
开始；新加入者，接受初步知识者
新加入的；接受初步知识的

morphology.mnemonic: 开始/初始 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French domestique (14c.) and directly from Latin domesticus "belonging to the household," from domus "house," from PIE *dom-o- "house," from root *dem- "house, household" (source also of Sanskrit damah "house;" Avestan demana- "house;" Greek domos "house," despotes "master, lord;" Latin dominus "master of a household;" Old Church Slavonic domu, Russian dom "house;" Lithuanian dimstis "enclosed court, property;" Old Norse topt "homestead").

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 坚固/稳定 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: mid-15c., "sick bay in a monastery," formerly also enfermerie, also firmary, fermery, from Old French enfermerie "hospital" and directly from Medieval Latin infirmaria "a place for the infirm," from Latin infirmus "weak, frail," (see infirm).
in reference to jobs going overseas, by 1981 (as outsourcing), from out + source (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 陆地的， 陆生的， 陆栖的； 地球的

etymology.story: late 14c., "of or pertaining to the earth," with + -al (1) + from Latin terrestris "earthly, of the earth, on land," from terra "earth" (see terrain).

morphology.mnemonic: ir + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程
