etymology.story: 1610s, "a supplying of water to land," also in medical use, "supply of a liquid to some part of the body," from Latin irrigationem (nominative irrigatio) "a watering, irrigation," noun of action from past participle stem of irrigare "lead water to, irrigate, flood" (see irrigate)..

meanings.definitionCn: 编译；编制；编辑；[图情] 汇编

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French compiler "compile, collect" (13c.), from Latin compilare "to plunder, rob," probably originally "bundle together, heap up;" hence "to pack up and carry off," from com- "together" (see com-) + pilare "to compress, ram down."
Old English smelt "sardine, small salmon-like sea fish," cognate with Dutch smelt "sand eel," Danish smelt (c.
1610s, "pertaining to movement," from French locomotif, from Latin loco "from a place" (ablative of locus "place;" see locus) + Late Latin motivus "moving" (see motive).

1570s, from Latin inhaerentem (nominative inhaerens), present participle of inhaerere "be closely connected with, be inherent," literally "adhere to, cling to," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + haerere "to stick" (see hesitation).

meanings.definitionCn: 辐射；传播；流露；发射；广播

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "having rays, furnished with rays, shining," 1660s, from Latin radiatus (see radiate (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 侵入， 侵占， 侵犯； 侵蚀， 蚕食

etymology.story: late 14c., "acquire, get," from Old French encrochier "seize, fasten on, hang on (to), cling (to); hang up, suspend," literally "to catch with a hook," from en- "in" (see en- (1)) + croc "hook," from Old Norse krokr "hook" (see crook (n.)).
"spoken," 1570s, past participle adjective from pronounce (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 规定， 明确要求； 讲定， 约定

etymology.story: 1620s, "bargain, make a contract" (intransitive), back-formation from stipulation, or else from Latin stipulatus, past participle of stipulari "exact (a promise), bargain for." Transitive sense of "demand as a condition" is from 1640s.

late 15c., with -y (2) + ware, from Old English wær "prudent, aware, alert, wary," from Proto-Germanic *waraz (source also of Old Norse varr "attentive," Gothic wars "cautious," Old Saxon giwar, Middle Dutch gheware, Old High German giwar, German gewahr "aware"), from PIE root *wer- (4) "to perceive, watch out for" (see ward (n.)).
1650s, from Late Latin ultimatus, past participle of ultimare "to be final, come to an end," from Latin ultimus (fem.
early 15c., "follow up, pursue" (some course or action), from Latin prosecutus, past participle of prosequi "follow after, accompany; chase, pursue; attack, assail, abuse" (see pursue).
Old English *bæcce "something baked," from bacan "bake" (see bake (v.)).

late 15c., "interior, inward, internal," from Middle French intrinsèque "inner" (14c.), from Medieval Latin intrinsecus "interior, internal," from Latin intrinsecus (adv.) "inwardly, on the inside," from intra "within" (see intra-) + secus "along, alongside," from PIE *sekw-os- "following," suffixed form of root *sekw- (1) "to follow" (see sequel).

root.meaning: money given for labor or services, wages

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + money given for labor or services, wages

etymology.story: also pre-pay, 1839, originally of postage, from pre- + pay (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: n中值，中位数；三角形中线；梯形中位线

etymology.story: "a median part," 1540s, from Latin medianus (see median (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 接近， 邻近； 亲近

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French proximité "nearness" (14c.), from Latin proximitatem (nominative proximitas) "nearness, vicinity," from proximus "nearest, next; most direct; adjoining," figuratively "latest, most recent; next, following; most faithful," superlative of prope "near" (see propinquity)..

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 星 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1620s, "situated between or among the stars," in reference to the night sky (modern astronomical sense is from 1670s), from inter- "between" + Latin stella "star" (see stellar).

1610s, "territory under the jurisdiction of a lord or officer," from French district (16c.), from Medieval Latin districtus "restraining of offenders, jurisdiction," then under the feudal system "area of jurisdiction," noun use of past participle of Latin distringere "hinder, detain" (see distress).
early 15c., "full of light," from Latin luminosus "shining, full of light," from lumen (genitive luminis) "light," related to lucere "to shine" (see light (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 树木的； 栖于树木的

etymology.story: 1660s, from Latin arboreus "pertaining to trees," from arbor "tree," which is of unknown origin, + -al (1)..

morphology.mnemonic: 过度/超越 + wholly, without limit

etymology.story: "everywhere," Old English ofer eall, from ofer "over" (see over) + eall (see all).

meanings.definitionCn: 海的， 海事的； 海运的

etymology.story: 1540s, "of or pertaining to the sea," from Middle French maritime (16c.) or directly from Latin maritimus "of the sea, near the sea," from mare (genitive maris) "sea" (see mere (n.)) + Latin ending -timus, originally a superlative suffix (compare intimus "inmost," ultimus "last"), here denoting "close association with." Maritimes "seacoast regions of a country" is from 1590s; specifically of the southeasternmost provinces of Canada by 1926..
late 14c., "act of receiving;" also "statement of ingredients in a potion or medicine;" from Anglo-French or Old North French receite "receipt, recipe, prescription" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 假装；假定，设想；承担；呈现，采取

etymology.story: early 15c., assumpten "to receive up into heaven" (especially of the Virgin Mary), also assumen "to arrogate," from Latin assumere, adsumere "to take up, take to oneself, take besides, obtain in addition," from ad- "to, up" (see ad-) + sumere "to take," from sub "under" (see sub-) + emere "to take" (see exempt (adj.)).

1847, "a submitting of a question to the voters as a whole" (originally chiefly in reference to Switzerland), from French or German, from Latin referendum "that which must be referred," literally "thing brought back," neuter gerundive of referre "to bring or take back" (see refer).
1650s, from French capsule "a membranous sac" (16c.), from Latin capsula "small box or chest," diminutive of capsa "box, case, chest" (see case (n.2)).

"a ludicrous trick" [Johnson], 1520s, of uncertain origin, perhaps related to obsolete verb prank "decorate, dress up" (mid-15c.), related to Middle Low German prank "display" (compare also Dutch pronken, German prunken "to make a show, to strut").

meanings.definitionCn: 一千年， 千禧年； 太平盛世

etymology.story: 1630s, from Modern Latin millennium, from Latin mille "thousand" (see million) + annus "year" (see annual); formed on analogy of biennium, triennium, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 穷困的， 赤贫的； 贫乏的， 贫瘠的； 枯竭的
难以防守的； 不能租赁的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + 持有/保持 + able

etymology.story: 1640s, "indefensible, incapable of being held against attack," from un- (1) "not" + tenable (adj.).
1840, from German Ozon, coined in 1840 by German chemist Christian Friedrich Schönbein (1799-1868) from Greek ozon, neuter present participle of ozein "to smell" (see odor).

meanings.definitionCn: 鬼怪， 幽灵； 缠绕心头的恐惧， 凶兆

etymology.story: 1590s, from Middle French désastre (1560s), from Italian disastro "ill-starred," from dis-, here merely pejorative (see dis-) + astro "star, planet," from Latin astrum, from Greek astron (see star (n.)).

1873, oecology, "branch of science dealing with the relationship of living things to their environments," coined in German by German zoologist Ernst Haeckel (1834-1919) as Ökologie, from Greek oikos "house, dwelling place, habitation" (see villa) + -logia "study of" (see -logy).
late 14c., from Old French prudent "with knowledge, deliberate" (c.
1707, back-formation from secretion.

late 13c., anhaunsen "to raise, make higher," from Anglo-French enhauncer, probably from Old French enhaucier "make greater, make higher or louder; fatten, foster; raise in esteem," from Vulgar Latin *inaltiare, from Late Latin inaltare "raise, exalt,"  from altare "make high," from altus "high" (see old).

early 14c., from en- (1) + close, and partially from Old French enclos, past participle of enclore "surround; confine; contain." Specific sense of "to fence in waste or common ground" for the purpose of cultivation or to give it to private owners is from c.
"survey, summary," 1934, American English, from over- + view (n.).
"to place (two or more objects) side by side or close together," 1826, a back-formation from juxtaposition or else from French juxtaposer (18c.).

meanings.definitionCn: 一般的， 普通的， 通用的； 种的， 属的

etymology.story: 1670s, "belonging to a large group of objects," formed in English from Latin gener-, stem of genus "race, kind" (see genus) + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 赞同， 认可；代言；背书； 在背面签名<
倾泻； 充满， 遍布
验光师， 视力测定者

etymology.story: 1903; see optometry + -ist..
"indicate beforehand," 1570s, figurative, from fore- + shadow (v.); the notion seems to be a shadow thrown before an advancing material object as an image of something suggestive of what is to come.

meanings.definitionCn: 基础设施；公共建设；下部构造

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下/基础 + 建造 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: 1887, from French infrastructure (1875); see infra- + structure (n.).
1550s, from Middle French récipient (16c.) and directly from Latin recipientem (nominative recipiens), present participle of recipere (see receive).

late 14c., from Old French replet "filled up" (14c.), from Latin repletus "filled, full," past participle of replere "to fill; fill again, re-fill," from re- (see re-) + plere "to fill" (see pleio-)..

"order forbidding ships from certain other nations from entering or leaving a nation's ports," 1590s, from Spanish embargo "seizure, arrest; embargo," noun of action from embargar "restrain, impede, arrest, embargo," from Vulgar Latin *imbarricare, from assimilated form of in- "into, upon" (see in- (2)) + *barra (see bar (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 夜晚的； 夜晚发生的； 夜晚活动的

morphology.mnemonic: 夜 + urn + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 15c., from Old French nocturnal "nightly, nocturnal," or directly from Late Latin nocturnalis, from Latin nocturnus "belonging to the night," from nox (genitive noctis) "night," cognate with Old English neaht (see night) + -urnus, suffix forming adjectives of time.

meanings.definitionCn: 腐蚀；腐蚀产生的物质；衰败
声名狼藉的，臭名昭著的

etymology.story: 1540s, "publicly known," from Medieval Latin notorius "well-known, commonly known," from Latin notus "known," past participle of noscere "come to know" (see know).

meanings.definitionCn: 令人发腻， 失去吸引力

etymology.story: "become tiresome," 1700, from Middle English pallen "to become faint, fail in strength" (late 14c.), shortened form of appallen "to dismay, fill with horror or disgust" (see appall).

1530, "goat sent into the wilderness on the Day of Atonement, symbolic bearer of the sins of the people," coined by Tyndale from scape (n.1) + goat to translate Latin caper emissarius, itself a translation in Vulgate of Hebrew 'azazel (Lev.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 站立/设置 + ancy

etymology.story: 1590s, "firmness of matter," from Medieval Latin consistentia or directly from Latin consistentem, from consistere (see consist).

1821, artefact, "anything made by human art," from Italian artefatto, from Latin arte "by skill" (ablative of ars "art;" see art (n.)) + factum "thing made," from facere "to make, do" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 引出，预先提出；作为…的前提
前提；上述各项；房屋连地基

etymology.story: 1620s, "to settle with colonists," from stem of Latin colonus "tiller of the soil, farmer" (see colony); in sense "to make another place into a national dependency" without regard for settlement there by 1790s (such as in reference to French activity in Egypt or British work in India), and probably directly from colony.
1580s, from Late Latin controversialis "pertaining to controversy," from Latin controversia (see controversy)..
1841, coined in Modern Latin by Sir Richard Owen, from comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 承租人；房客；佃户；居住者

morphology.mnemonic: 持有/保持 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 14c., "person who holds lands by title or by lease," from Anglo-French tenaunt (late 13c.), Old French tenant "possessor; feudal tenant" (12c.), noun use of present participle of tenir "to hold," from Latin tenere "hold, keep, grasp" (see tenet).

morphology.mnemonic: 脚/儿童 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "subject to taxation," late 15c., from Anglo-French taxable, Anglo-Latin taxabilis; see tax (v.) + -able.

meanings.definitionCn: 象限；[海洋][天] 象限仪；四分之一圆

etymology.story: late 14c., "a quarter of a day, six hours," from Middle French quadrant, from Latin quadrantem (nominative quadrans) "fourth part," also the name of a coin worth a quarter of an as, noun use of present participle of quadrare "to make square; put in order, arrange, complete; run parallel, be exact," figuratively "to fit, suit, be proper," related to quadrus "a square," quattuor "four" (see four).

"particular to a people or locality," 1650s (endemical), with -ic + Greek endemos "native, dwelling in (a place), of or belonging to a people," from en "in" (see en- (2)) + demos "people, district" (see demotic).

late 14c., "condition of babyhood," also "childhood, youth," from Anglo-French enfaunce and directly from Latin infantia "early childhood," from infantem "young child," literally "one unable to speak" (see infant).

meanings.definitionCn: 最高的；至高的；最重要的

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French suprême (15c.) and directly from Latin supremus "highest," superlative of superus "situated above," from super "above" (see super-).
late 14c., "state of being earlier," from Old French priorite (14c.), from Medieval Latin prioritatem (nominative prioritas) "fact or condition of being prior," from Latin prior (see prior (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 取出；抽出；拔出；抽出物；出身

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 拉/吸引 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "process of withdrawing or obtaining" (something, from something else), from Old French estraction "extraction, origin" (12c.) or directly from Medieval Latin extractionem (nominative extractio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin extrahere "to draw out" (see extract (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 斜坡；倾斜；斜率；扛枪姿势

morphology.mnemonic: 爱 + 具有…特征/名词化

meanings.definitionCn: 引进; 嵌入; 插入;

etymology.story: "narrow opening into a coast, arm of the sea," 1570s, said by old sources to be originally a Kentish term; a special use of Middle English inlate "passage or opening by which an enclosed place may be entered" (c.
1560s, from Middle French métallique or directly from Latin metallicus, from Greek metallikos, from metallon (see metal)..
1670s, "having the quality of creating," from create + -ive.
1630s, from Latin praevent-, past participle stem of praevenire (see prevent), + -ive.

early 15c., "exposition of myths," from Middle French mythologie and directly from Late Latin mythologia, from Greek mythologia "legendary lore, a telling of mythic legends; a legend, story, tale," from mythos "myth" (of unknown origin) + -logy "study." Meaning "a body of myths" first recorded 1781..

meanings.definitionCn: 公正的，明断的；法庭的；审判上的

etymology.story: late 14c., "of or pertaining to a judge; pertaining to the administration of justice," from Latin iudicalis "of or belonging to a court of justice," from iudicium "judgment, decision of a court of justice," also the court itself, from iudex "a judge" (see judge (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 航天飞机；穿梭；梭子；穿梭班机、公共汽车等
使穿梭般来回移动；短程穿梭般运送

etymology.story: Old English scytel "a dart, arrow," from Proto-Germanic *skutilaz (source also of Old Norse skutill "harpoon"), from PIE *skeud- "to shoot, to chase, to throw, to project" (see shoot (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 敷衍的；马虎的；得过且过的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Late Latin perfunctorius "careless, negligent," literally "like one who wishes to get through a thing," from Latin perfungus, past participle of perfungi "discharge, busy oneself, get through," from per- "through" + fungi "perform" (see function (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + gener + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1550s, back-formation from regeneration or else from Latin regeneratus, past participle of regenerare "bring forth again" (see regeneration).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 地方 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1630s, from verbal used of adjective allocate (mid-15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 之前, 先(用先发制人的方法)预防, 阻止

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 射线/放射 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin eradicationem (nominative eradicatio), noun of action from past participle stem of eradicare "root out, extirpate, annihilate," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + radix (genitive radicis) "root" (see radish)..

meanings.definitionCn: 壁龛,合适的职务(环境、位置等)

etymology.story: 1610s, "shallow recess in a wall," from French niche "recess (for a dog), kennel" (14c.), perhaps from Italian nicchia "niche, nook," from nicchio "seashell," said by Klein and Barnhart to be probably from Latin mitulus "mussel," but the change of -m- to -n- is not explained.

morphology.mnemonic: 反对/相反 + 生命/生物 + ic

meanings.definitionCn: 最初的,初级的;首要的,主要的,基本的

morphology.mnemonic: 第一/首要 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1861, American English, short for primary election (1792, with reference to France; in a U.S.

meanings.definitionCn: 海盗；盗版；侵犯专利权者
喘息；渴望；气喘吁吁地说出某事
事业,企(事)业单位;事业心,进取心

etymology.story: early 15c., "an undertaking," formerly also enterprize, from Old French enterprise "an undertaking," noun use of fem.

morphology.mnemonic: 独自/太阳 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "pertaining to the sun," from Latin solaris "of the sun," from sol "sun" (see sol).

meanings.definitionCn: 形成;构成;组织;构造;编制;塑造

morphology.mnemonic: 形状/形态 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "vital force in plants and animals;" early 15c., "act of creating or making," from Old French formacion "formation, fashioning, creation" (12c.) or directly from Latin formationem (nominative formatio) "a forming, shaping," noun of action or condition from past participle stem of formare "to form," from forma "form, shape" (see form (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 移动/迁移 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1778, a back-formation from emigration, or else from Latin emigratus, past participle of emigrare "move away." In 19c.

meanings.definitionCn: 外国的；相异的，性质不同的；不相容的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Late Latin benefactor, from Latin phrase bene facere, from bene "well" (see bene-) + facere "to do" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 三角（形）；三角关系；三角形之物；三人一组

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French triangle (13c.), from Latin triangulum "triangle," noun use of neuter of adjective triangulus "three-cornered, having three angles," from tri- "three" (see tri-) + angulus "corner, angle" (see angle (n.))..

"combustible cord or tube for lighting an explosive device," also fuze, 1640s, from Italian fuso, literally "spindle" (the ignition device so called for its shape, because the originals were long, thin tubes filled with gunpowder), from Latin fusus "a spindle," which is of uncertain origin.
1590s, "rivalry" (based on compete); c.
"a choice or select body, the best part," 1823, from French élite "selection, choice," from Old French eslite (12c.), fem.
early 15c., "in large quantities," from whole (adj.) + sale; the general sense of "extensive" is attested from 1640s.

meanings.definitionCn: 额外费用；奖金；保险费;(商)溢价
牧歌的；牧羊的；乡下风味的

etymology.story: 1610s, earlier bucolical (1520s), from Latin bucolicus, from Greek boukolikos "pastoral, rustic," from boukolos "cowherd, herdsman," from bous "cow" (see cow (n.)) + -kolos "tending," related to Latin colere "to till (the ground), cultivate, dwell, inhabit" (the root of colony).

1829, from French solidarité "communion of interests and responsibilities, mutual responsibility," a coinage of the "Encyclopédie" (1765), from solidaire "interdependent, complete, entire," from solide (see solid (adj.)).

early 14c., "subject or topic on which a person writes or speaks," from Old French tesme (13c., with silent -s- "indicating vowel length" [OED], Modern French thème) and directly from Latin thema "a subject, thesis," from Greek thema "a proposition, subject, deposit," literally "something set down," from root of tithenai "put down, place," from PIE *dhe-mn, from root *dhe- "to put, to do" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 请教，向...咨询，找...商量；查阅，查看

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French consulter (16c.), from Latin consultare "consult, take the advice of," frequentative of consulere "to take counsel, meet and consider" (see consultation).

meanings.definitionCn: 选民, 选区, 有选举权者

etymology.story: 1670s, "condition of being an elector," in reference to Germany, from elector + -ate (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 据为己有, 占有, 挪用(只指定用途的一笔), 拨款

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + propri + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "taking (something) as private property," from Late Latin appropriationem (nominative appropriatio) "a making one's own," noun of action from past participle stem of appropriare (see appropriate).

late 13c., intice, from Old French enticier "to stir up (fire), to excite, incite," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps from Vulgar Latin *intitiare "set on fire," from Latin in- "in" (see in- (2)) + titio (genitive titionis) "firebrand," which is of uncertain origin.

late 14c., from Old French contradiction or directly from Latin contradictionem (nominative contradictio) "a reply, objection, counterargument," noun of action from past participle stem of contradicere, in classical Latin contra dicere "to speak against," from contra "against" (see contra) + dicere "to say, speak" (see diction)..
1520s, "sudden physical pain," of unknown origin, perhaps related to prong (prongys of deth is recorded from mid-15c.).

morphology.mnemonic: pred + …者/…器 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1580s, "involving plundering," from Latin praedatorius "pertaining to plunder," from praedator "plunderer," from praedor "to plunder," from praeda "prey" (see prey (n.)).
1680s, "greater weight," from Latin praeponderans, present participle of praeponderare "make heavier" (see preponderate).
1570s, from Middle French sentinelle (16c.), from Italian sentinella "a sentinel." OED says "No convincing etymology of the It.

morphology.mnemonic: 开始/初始 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 有效的,效率高的;有能力的,能胜任的

etymology.story: late 14c., "making, producing immediate effect, active, effective," from Old French efficient and directly from Latin efficientem (nominative efficiens) "effective, efficient, producing, active," present participle of efficere "work out, accomplish" (see effect).

meanings.definitionCn: 基本的， 初步的； 未充分发展的， 发育不成熟的； 退化的

morphology.mnemonic: 原始，粗野 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1827; see rudiment + -ary.
1882, in biology, from stem of symbiosis + -ic.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1570s, "standing firm, standing still," from Latin consistentem (nominative consistens), present participle of consistere (see consist).

meanings.definitionCn: 大批出动，成群出现(鼠类及害虫)

etymology.story: late 15c., "to attack, assail, hurt, distress, annoy," from Old French infester (14c.), from Latin infestare "to attack, disturb, trouble," from infestus "unsafe, hostile, threatening, dangerous," originally "inexorable, not able to be handled," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + -festus "(able to be) seized." Sense of "swarm over in large numbers, attack parasitically" first recorded c.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 取/抓 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1820, "pertaining to mental conception" (there is an isolated use from 1662), from Medieval Latin conceptualis, from Latin conceptus "a collecting, gathering, conceiving," past participle of concipere (see conceive).

meanings.definitionCn: 贫瘠的；不育的；(植物)不结果的；无用的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French eternel "eternal," or directly from Late Latin aeternalis, from Latin aeternus "of an age, lasting, enduring, permanent, everlasting, endless," contraction of aeviternus "of great age," from aevum "age" (see eon).

meanings.definitionCn: 救济；赔偿；纠正；重新调整

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to correct, reform;" late 14c., "restore, put right" (a wrong, error, offense); "repair; relieve; improve; amend," from Old French redrecier "reform, restore, rebuild" (Modern French redresser), from re- "again" (see re-) + drecier "to straighten, arrange" (see dress (v.)).
Old English graf "grove, copse, small wood" (akin to græafa "thicket"), not certainly found in other Germanic languages and with no known cognates.

meanings.definitionCn: 古， 古老； 古代； 古人； 古迹， 古物

etymology.story: late 14c., "olden times," from Old French antiquitet (11c.; Modern French antiquité) "olden times; great age; old age," from Latin antiquitatem (nominative antiquitas) "ancient times, antiquity, venerableness," noun of quality from antiquus (see antique (adj.)).

early 15c., from Middle French permanent (14c.) or directly from Latin permanentem (nominative permanens) "remaining," present participle of permanere "endure, hold out, continue, stay to the end," from per- "through"  (see per) + manere "stay" (see mansion).

Middle English fecchen, from Old English feccan "to bring, bring to; seek, gain, take," apparently a variant of fetian, fatian "bring near, bring back, obtain; induce; marry," which is probably from Proto-Germanic *fetan (source also of Old Frisian fatia "to grasp, seize, contain," Old Norse feta "to find one's way," Middle Dutch vatten, Old High German sih faggon "to mount, climb," German fassen "to grasp, contain").

meanings.definitionCn: 培养；养育，抚育；抱（希望等）
(Foster)人名；(英、捷、意、葡、法、德、俄、西)福斯特

etymology.story: Old English *fostrian "to supply with food, nourish, support," from fostor "food, nourishment, bringing up," from Proto-Germanic *fostra-, from extended form of PIE root *pa- "to protect; feed" (see food).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 强壮/价值 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 15c., "having great power or force," from Latin praevalentem (nominative praevalens) "of superior strength; mighty," present participle of praevalere "to be more able" (see prevail).

early 15c., "gentleness, lightness," from Middle French facilité "easiness, ease," from Latin facilitatem (nominative facilitas) "easiness, ease, fluency, willingness," from facilis "easy" (see facile).

meanings.definitionCn: 群居的； 合群的； 爱交际的

etymology.story: 1660s, "disposed to live in flocks" (of animals), from Latin gregarius "pertaining to a flock; of the herd, of the common sort, common," from grex (genitive gregis) "flock, herd," from PIE *gre-g-, reduplicated form of root *ger- (1) "to gather together, assemble" (source also of Sanskrit gramah "heap, troop;" Greek ageirein "to assemble," agora "assembly;" Latin gremium "bosom, lap;" Old Church Slavonic grusti "handful," gramota "heap;" Lithuanian gurgulys "chaos, confusion," gurguole "crowd, mass").

meanings.definitionCn: 横梁；光线；电波；船宽；[计量] 秤杆
发送；以梁支撑；用…照射；流露

etymology.story: "a nutritious substance," 1828, noun use of adjective (1640s) meaning "providing nourishment," from Latin nutrientem (nominative nutriens), present participle of nutrire "to nourish, suckle, feed, foster" (see nourish)..

morphology.mnemonic: 公民 + ize + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1704, "law which makes a criminal process civil," from civilize + -ation.
early 15c., from Latin compactus, past participle of compingere "to fasten together" (see compact (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 亡命之徒，逃犯，被剥夺法律权益者
宣布…为非法， 使…成为非法

etymology.story: Old English utlagian "to outlaw, banish," from utlaga "an outlaw" (see outlaw (n.)).
"act or fact of laying out (especially money) or expending," 1798, originally Scottish, from out (adv.) + lay (v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 航空；飞行术；飞机制造业

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1866, from French aviation, noun of action from stem of Latin avis "bird" (see aviary).

meanings.definitionCn: 与…竞争， 赶上； 仿真， 模仿

etymology.story: 1580s, back-formation from emulation, or else from Latin aemulatus, past participle of aemulari "to rival."
1530s, "to test, try, learn by practical trial or proof;" see experience (n.).
"combine or bring together" (two or more things), 1825, from synthesis + -ize.
late 14c., "to prostrate oneself," also "to fall flat," from flat (adj.) + -en (1).
1813, from mammal + -ian.

meanings.definitionCn: 压碎， 挤压； 挤进， 塞入； 镇住， 镇压； 制止

etymology.story: "to crush, squeeze," early 14c., squachen, from Old French esquasser, escasser "to crush, shatter, destroy, break," from Vulgar Latin *exquassare, from Latin ex- "out" (see ex-) + quassare "to shatter" (see quash "to crush").
automatic instrument for regulating temperature, 1831, from thermo- + -stat..

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + notice, attention, reputation

etymology.story: 1590s, from Middle French dénoter (14c.), from Latin denotare "denote, mark out," from de- "completely" (see de-) + notare "to mark" (see note (v.)).

1580s, "apostate," probably (with change of suffix) from Spanish renegado, originally "Christian turned Muslim," from Medieval Latin renegatus, noun use of past participle of renegare "deny" (see renege).

1570s, "intensity of expression," from Latin emphasis, from Greek emphasis "an appearing in, outward appearance;" in rhetoric, "significance, indirect meaning," from emphainein "to present, exhibit, display, let (a thing) be seen; be reflected (in a mirror), become visible," from assimilated form of en "in" (see en- (2)) + phainein "to show" (see phantasm).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 写 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin inscriptionem (nominative inscriptio) "a writing upon, inscription," noun of action from past participle stem of inscribere "inscribe, to write on or in (something)," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + scribere "to write" (see script (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 繁殖， 增殖； 传播， 宣传， 使普及

etymology.story: 1560s, "to cause to multiply," from Latin propagatus, past participle of propagare "to set forward, extend, procreate" (see propagation).

morphology.mnemonic: un + 状态/行为/过程 + ize + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 可行的；可能的；可实行的

etymology.story: "capable of being done, accomplished or carried out," mid-15c., from Anglo-French faisible, from Old French faisable "possible, that may be done; easy, convenient," from fais-, stem of faire "do, make," from Latin facere "to make, do, perform" (see factitious).
"to write down as a direction," mid-15c., from Latin praescribere "write beforehand" (see prescription).

meanings.definitionCn: 潜能；可能性；[电] 电势

morphology.mnemonic: 力量/能力 + …的人/处于…状态 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "possible" (as opposed to actual), from Old French potenciel and directly from Late Latin potentialis "potential," from Latin potentia "power, might, force;" figuratively "political power, authority, influence," from potens "powerful" (see potent).

meanings.definitionCn: 正统的；传统的；惯常的；东正教的

etymology.story: mid-15c., of opinions, faith, from Late Latin orthodoxus, from Greek orthodoxos "having the right opinion," from orthos "right, true, straight" (see ortho-) + doxa "opinion, praise," from dokein "to seem," from PIE root *dek- "to take, accept" (see decent).
1620s, from Medieval Latin necessitatus, past participle of necessitare "to render necessary," from Latin necessitas (see necessity).

meanings.definitionCn: 惯常的；习惯的，适应的(一般作表语)

etymology.story: late 15c., "made customary, habitual," past participle adjective from accustom (v.)..

1640s, "animating spirit," from Latin psyche, from Greek psykhe "the soul, mind, spirit; breath; life, one's life, the invisible animating principle or entity which occupies and directs the physical body; understanding" (personified as Psykhe, the beloved of Eros), akin to psykhein "to blow, cool," from PIE root *bhes- "to blow, to breathe" (source also of Sanskrit bhas-), "Probably imitative" [Watkins].

meanings.definitionCn: 违法交易, 违禁品, 走私

etymology.story: 1520s, "smuggling;" 1590s, "smuggled goods;" from Middle French contrebande "a smuggling," from older Italian contrabando (modern contrabbando) "unlawful dealing," from Latin contra "against" (see contra) + Medieval Latin bannum, from Frankish *ban "a command" or some other Germanic source (see ban (v.))..
"an expert," especially "one who is skilled in the secrets of anything," 1660s, from Latin adeptus (see adept (adj.)).
1550s, from Middle French quadruple (13c.), from Latin quadruplus "fourfold," from quadri- "four" (see quadru-) + -plus "-fold" (see -plus)..

meanings.definitionCn: 休息室；闲逛；躺椅；（英）酒吧间
闲逛；懒洋洋地躺卧；闲混

etymology.story: late 14c., "careful attention to detail," also "desire to know or learn" (originally usually in a bad sense), from Old French curiosete "curiosity, avidity, choosiness" (Modern French curiosité), from Latin curiositatem (nominative curiositas) "desire of knowledge, inquisitiveness," from curiosus (see curious).

meanings.definitionCn: 抵抗,反抗;抗,忍得住,抵制

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 站立/设置

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French resister "hold out against" (14c.), from Latin resistere "to make a stand against, oppose; to stand back; withstand," from re- "against" (see re-) + sistere "take a stand, stand firm" (see assist).

meanings.definitionCn: 分配；分配物；养家费；命运

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1570s, "action of allotting," from Middle French allotement, from Old French aloter (see allot).

meanings.definitionCn: 特殊的,苛求的,个别的n.详情,细节,特色

morphology.mnemonic: 平等 + 与…有关的/像…的

meanings.definitionCn: [法] 非法的；违法的；违反规则的

etymology.story: 1630s, from French illégal (14c.) or directly from Medieval Latin illegalis, from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + Latin legalis (see legal).
late 13c., cors "body," from Old French cors "body, person, corpse, life" (9c.), from Latin corpus "body" (see corporeal).
1610s, "rouse to action," from Latin stimulatus, past participle of stimulare (see stimulation).

root.meaning: stand in some relation; have reference or respect

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + stand in some relation; have reference or respect

etymology.story: 1742, back-formation from correlation, or else a verbal use of the noun.
early 15c., "vigor," from Middle French animosité (14c.) or directly from Latin animositatem (nominative animositas) "boldness, vehemence," from animosus "bold, spirited," from animus (see animus).
1670s, from counter- + act (v.).
mid-15c., from Middle French ample, from Latin amplus "large, spacious," related to ampla "handle, grip.".
late 14c., from Old French essorer "fly up, soar," from Vulgar Latin *exaurare "rise into the air," from Latin ex- "out" (see ex-) + aura "breeze, air" (see aura).
mid-15c., "to change, transform," from Latin commutare "to often change, to change altogether," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + mutare "to change" (see mutable).

meanings.definitionCn: 维持；支撑，承担；忍受；供养；证实

etymology.story: 1782, from French classifier, from classe (see class (n.)) + -fier (see -fy).
late 14c., from Latin altitudinem (nominative altitudo) "height, altitude," from altus "high" (see old)..

meanings.definitionCn: 民族志学的,  人种志学的
代替， 置换； 代入法

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + 建立/放置 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "appointment of a subordinate or successor," from Middle French substitution or directly from Late Latin substitutionem (nominative substitutio) "a putting in place of (another)," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin substituere "put in place of another, place under or next to, present, submit," from sub "under" (see sub-) + statuere "set up," from PIE root *stā- "to stand," with derivatives meaning "place or thing that is standing" (see stet)..

meanings.definitionCn: 确定， 确信； 必然性

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French certitude "certainty" (16c.), from Late Latin certitudinem (nominative certitudo) "that which is certain," from Latin certus "sure, certain" (see certain)..
Old English oferyrnan; see over- + run (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 批评的,评论的;危急,紧要的;临界的

root.meaning: one who judges merits of books, plays, etc.

morphology.mnemonic: one who judges merits of books, plays, etc. + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1580s, "censorious," from critic + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: [化工] 反应器；[核] 反应堆；起反应的人

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 做/驱动 + …者/…器

etymology.story: "one that reacts," 1835, agent noun in Latin form from react.

meanings.definitionCn: 奖学金；固定薪金；定期津贴；养老金

etymology.story: early 15c., "periodical payment; soldier's pay," from Latin stipendium "tax, impost, tribute," in military use "pay, salary," from stips "alms, small payment, contribution of money, gift" + pendere "weigh" (see pendant).

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + 看见 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1914, from en- (1) "make, put in" + vision (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 辐射的；容光焕发的；光芒四射的

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: "point or object from which light radiates," 1727; see radiant (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 穿过， 渗入；看穿， 洞察

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin penetratus, past participle of penetrare "to put or get into, enter into," related to penitus "within, inmost," penus "innermost part of a temple, store of food," penates "household gods."
1620s, from homogene "of the same kind" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 最重要的,主要的n.负责人,校长;资本,本金

etymology.story: 1610s, "pattern, model," from Latin specimen "indication, mark, example, sign, evidence; that by which a thing is known, means of knowing," from specere "to look at" (see scope (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 异国的；外来的；异国情调的

etymology.story: 1590s, "belonging to another country," from Middle French exotique (16c.) and directly from Latin exoticus, from Greek exotikos "foreign," literally "from the outside," from exo "outside" (see exo-).
1880, noun and verb, from Irish Land League ostracism of Capt.

meanings.definitionCn: 方面;[数] 维；尺寸；次元；容积 vt标出尺寸

etymology.story: late 14c., "measurement, size," from Latin dimensionem (nominative dimensio) "a measuring," noun of action from past participle stem of dimetri "to measure out," from dis- (see dis-) + metiri "to measure" (see measure (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 保持/服务

etymology.story: early 13c., from Old French deservir (Modern French desservir) "deserve, be worthy of, earn, merit," from Latin deservire "serve well," from de- "completely" (see de-) + servire "to serve" (see serve).

meanings.definitionCn: 过程,进程;工序,制作法;工艺

etymology.story: early 14c., "fact of being carried on" (as in in process), from Old French proces "a journey; continuation, development; legal trial" (13c.) and directly from Latin processus "a going forward, advance, progress," from past participle stem of procedere "go forward" (see proceed).

early 15c., "to sign at the bottom of a document," from Latin subscribere "write, write underneath, sign one's name; register," also figuratively "assent, agree to, approve," from sub "underneath" (see sub-) + scribere "write" (see script (n.)).
also distil, late 14c., from Old French distiller (14c.), from Latin distillare "trickle down in minute drops," from dis- "apart" (see dis-) + stillare "to drip, drop," from stilla "drop."

1846, "medical treatment of disease," from Modern Latin therapia, from Greek therapeia "curing, healing, service done to the sick; a waiting on, service," from therapeuein "to cure, treat medically," literally "attend, do service, take care of" (see therapeutic)..

meanings.definitionCn: 供选择的；选择性的；交替的
二中择一；供替代的选择

morphology.mnemonic: altern + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1620s, in rhetoric, from Medieval Latin alternativus (see alternative (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 无价值的， 微不足道的

etymology.story: 1560s, probably an adjectival use of noun paltry "worthless thing" (1550s), associated with dialectal palt, pelt "trash," cognate with Middle Low German and East Frisian palte "rag," Middle Dutch palt "broken or torn fragment." Similar formation in Low German paltrig "rubbishy," East Frisian palterig "ragged, torn.".

meanings.definitionCn: 浓度；集中；浓缩；专心；集合

root.meaning: to rest as at a center

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + to rest as at a center + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1630s, "action of bringing to a center," noun of action from verb concentrate (v.).

tract of level or undulating grassland in North America, by 1773, from French prairie "meadow, grassland," from Old French praerie "meadow, pastureland" (12c.), from Vulgar Latin *prataria, from Latin pratum "meadow," originally "a hollow." The word existed in Middle English as prayere, but was lost and reborrowed to describe the American plains.

morphology.mnemonic: du + 折叠/交织 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "doubling," from Middle French duplicacion (13c.) and directly from Latin duplicationem (nominative duplicatio), noun of action from past participle stem of duplicare (see duplicate (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 土地的；耕地的；有关土地的

etymology.story: 1610s, "relating to the land," from Middle French loy agrarienne "agrarian law," corresponding to Latin Lex agraria, the Roman law for the division of conquered lands, from agrarius "of the land," from ager (genitive agri) "a field," from PIE *agro- (source also of Greek agros "field," Gothic akrs, Old English æcer "field;" see acre).

meanings.definitionCn: (of)赞成，赞许，同意；批准，审议，通过

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 做/驱动 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1812, from inter- + action..

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + one who goes with another.

etymology.story: formerly also enterfere, mid-15c., "to strike against," from Middle French enterferir "exchange blows, strike each other," from entre- "between" (see entre-) + ferir "to strike," from Latin ferire "to knock, strike," related to Latin forare "to bore, pierce" (see bore (v.), and compare punch (v.), which has both the senses "to hit" and "to make a hole in").

meanings.definitionCn: 出席；到场；出席人数；考勤

etymology.story: late 14c., "act of attending to one's duties," from Old French atendance "attention, wait, hope, expectation," from atendant, present participle of atendre (see attend).

meanings.definitionCn: 分成地区, 按地区安排
无形的，触摸不到的；难以理解的

etymology.story: 1630s, "incapable of being touched," from French intangible (c.

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1898, from French radio-actif, coined by Pierre and Marie Curie from radio-, comb.

morphology.mnemonic: 不足/在…之下 + one's occupation, branch of business

etymology.story: 1721, "to mark underneath or below with a line," from under + line (v.).
"take (something) ill; be in some degree angry or provoked at," c.

mid-14c., from Old French compassion "sympathy, pity" (12c.), from Late Latin compassionem (nominative compassio) "sympathy," noun of state from past participle stem of compati "to feel pity," from com- "together" (see com-) + pati "to suffer" (see passion).
1660s, "dazzling," present participle adjective from stun (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 模糊不清的；引起歧义的

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin ambiguus "having double meaning, shifting, changeable, doubtful," adjective derived from ambigere "to dispute about," literally "to wander," from ambi- "about" (see ambi-) + agere "drive, lead, act" (see act (n.)).
Old English alecgan "to put down, remit, give up," a Germanic compound (cognates: Gothic uslagjan, Old High German irleccan, German erlegen), from a- "down, aside" + lecgan "to lay" (see lay (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 结合；装订；有约束力；过紧
捆绑；困境；讨厌的事情；植物的藤蔓
[药] 兴奋剂；刺激物；酒精饮料

etymology.story: 1772, from French stimulant or directly from Latin stimulantem (nominative stimulans), present participle of stimulare "to prick, urge, stimulate" (see stimulation).

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 向下/移除/取消 + 悬挂/称量/支付 + ance

etymology.story: 1816 (Coleridge), from inter- + dependence..
early 15c., "that produces a (physical) quality," from Medieval Latin qualitativus "relating to quality," from stem of Latin qualitas "a quality, property, nature" (see quality).

meanings.definitionCn: 使成立方形；使自乘二次；量…的体积
团结；重整；恢复；（网球等）连续对打
使同时发生，使同步发生

etymology.story: 1620s, "to occur at the same time," from Greek synkhronizein "be of the same time," from synkhronos "happening at the same time" (see synchronous).

meanings.definitionCn: 冲击， 碰撞；效果； 影响
侧面；轮廓；外形；剖面；简况

morphology.mnemonic: to esteem (something) more than others + ance

etymology.story: mid-15c., "advancement in position or status;" 1650s as "act of prefering," from Middle French preference (14c., Modern French préférence), from Medieval Latin preferentia, from past participle stem of Latin praeferrere (see prefer).

meanings.definitionCn: 主要倾向，主要趋势，主流；主流派爵士乐

etymology.story: also main-stream, main stream, "principal current of a river," 1660s, from main (adj.) + stream (n.); hence, "prevailing direction in opinion, popular taste, etc.," a figurative use first attested in Carlyle (1831).
1640s, back-formation from scavenger.

meanings.definitionCn: 瘫痪， 麻痹， 中风

morphology.mnemonic: 平等 + lys + 过程/状态

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin paralysis, from Greek paralysis "paralysis, palsy," literally "loosening," from paralyein "disable, enfeeble," from para- "beside" (see para- (1)) + lyein "loosen, untie" (see lose).
late 14c., from stiff (adj.) + -ness.
1580s, from Latin distortus, past participle of distorquere "to twist different ways, distort," from dis- "completely" + torquere "to twist" (see torque (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 次要的； 辅助的， 附设的

morphology.mnemonic: 沉降/补助 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1540s, from Latin subsidiarius "belonging to a reserve, of a reserve, reserved; serving to assist or supplement," from subsidium "a help, aid, relief, troops in reserve" (see subsidy).

meanings.definitionCn: 人口；[生物] 种群，[生物] 群体；全体居民

morphology.mnemonic: 人/民众 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1610s, from Late Latin populationem (nominative populatio) "a people; a multitude," as if from Latin populus "a people" (see people (n.)).

mid-13c., from Anglo-French buquet "bucket, pail," from Old French buquet "bucket," which is from Frankish or some other Germanic source, or a diminutive of cognate Old English buc "pitcher, bulging vessel," originally "belly" (buckets were formerly of leather as well as wood), both from West Germanic *buh- (source also of Dutch buik, Old High German buh, German Bauch "belly"), possibly from a variant of PIE root *beu-, *bheu- "to grow, swell" (see bull (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 腐蚀的, 蚀坏的 n腐蚀物

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French corrosif (13c.), from corroder (see corrode)..

late 14c., from Old French discerner (13c.) "distinguish (between), separate" (by sifting), and directly from Latin discernere "to separate, set apart, divide, distribute; distinguish, perceive," from dis- "off, away" (see dis-) + cernere "distinguish, separate, sift" (see crisis).

meanings.definitionCn: 灵活的；柔韧的；易弯曲的

etymology.story: early 15c., "capable of being bent; mentally or spiritually pliant," from Middle French flexible or directly from Latin flexibilis "that may be bent, pliant, flexible, yielding;" figuratively "tractable, inconstant," from flex-, past participle stem of flectere "to bend," which is of uncertain origin.
1610s, from Latin census "the enrollment of the names and property assessments of all Roman citizens," originally past participle of censere "to assess" (see censor (n.)).

early 15c., in Scottish, from Middle French domaine "domain, estate," from Old French demaine "lord's estate," from Latin dominium "property, dominion," from dominus "lord, master, owner," from domus "house" (see domestic).

meanings.definitionCn: 胡乱涂抹；弄脏，弄污；诽谤，诋毁；变得模糊不清，
相对的；有关系的；成比例的
亲戚；相关物；[语] 关系词；亲缘植物

root.meaning: stand in some relation; have reference or respect

morphology.mnemonic: stand in some relation; have reference or respect + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: early 15c., "having reference," from Middle French relatif and directly from Late Latin relativus (see relative (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 有责任的：顺从的，服从的；有义务的；经得起检验的

etymology.story: 1590s, "liable," from Anglo-French amenable, Middle French amener "answerable" (to the law), from à "to" (see ad-) + mener "to lead," from Latin minare "to drive (cattle) with shouts," variant of minari "threaten" (see menace (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + paint画，描绘

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin depictus, past participle of depingere "to portray, paint, sketch; describe, imagine," from de- "down" (see de-) + pingere "to paint" (see paint (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 终年的,四季不断的,持久的

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 年 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1640s, "evergreen," formed in English from Latin perennis "lasting through the year (or years)," from per- "through" (see per) + annus "year" (see annual).

meanings.definitionCn: 舒适；礼仪；愉快；便利设施

etymology.story: late 14c., "quality of being pleasant or agreeable," from Old French amenite, from Latin amoenitatem (nominative amoenitas) "delightfulness, pleasantness," from amoenus "pleasant," perhaps related to amare "to love" (see Amy)..
1848, "a period when business is down," from off (adv.) + season (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 炎症性的；煽动性的；激动的

morphology.mnemonic: in + to burn, set on fire + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: "tending to rouse passions or desires," 1711, a figurative use from Latin inflammat-, past participle stem of inflammare "to set on fire" (see inflame) + -ory.
1874, from link (v.) + -age..

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + a jump, a leap + er

etymology.story: 1590s, enterloper, "unauthorized trader trespassing on privileges of chartered companies," probably a hybrid from inter- "between" + -loper (from landloper "vagabond, adventurer," also, according to Johnson, "a term of reproach used by seamen of those who pass their lives on shore"); perhaps from a dialectal form of leap, or from Middle Dutch loper "runner, rover," from lopen "to run," from Proto-Germanic *hlaupan "to leap" (see leap (v.)).
"part of a country distant from the coast," 1796, from interior (adj.); meaning "internal part, inside" is from 1828.
1819, from medical Latin embryonem (see embryo) + -ic.

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French adopcion or directly from Latin adoptionem (nominative adoptio), noun of action from past participle stem of adoptare "chose for oneself, take by choice, select, adopt," especially "to take into a family, adopt as a child," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + optare "choose, wish, desire" (see option (n.))..

morphology.mnemonic: in + volve + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1706, from involve + -ment..
late 15c., "play or sing in harmony," from French harmoniser (15c.), from Old French harmonie (see harmony).
early 15c., from Old French distinctif and directly from Medieval Latin distinctivus, from Latin distinct-, past participle of distinguere (see distinguish).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + to shape into

etymology.story: early 15c., from Late Latin praefigurare "to prefigure," from Latin prae "before" (see pre-) + figurare "to form, shape," from figura "a shape, form, figure" (see figure (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 作品,作文,乐曲;作曲;结构,组成,成分

suffix.meaning: thing or animal spoken about before.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 放置 + thing or animal spoken about before. + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of combining," also "manner in which a thing is composed," from Old French composicion (13c., Modern French composition) "composition, make-up, literary work, agreement, settlement," from Latin compositionem (nominative compositio) "a putting together, connecting, arranging," noun of action from past participle stem of componere (see composite).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + quire + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "act of obtaining," from Old French acquisicion (13c.) or directly from Latin acquisitionem (nominative acquisitio), noun of action from past participle stem of acquirere "get in addition, accumulate," from ad- "extra" (see ad-) + quaerere "to seek to obtain" (see query (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 用碰(簧)锁锁上；用插销插上
严格的；僵硬的，死板的；坚硬的；精确的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin rigidus "hard, stiff, rough, severe," from rigere "be stiff," from PIE *reig- "stretch (tight), bind tightly, make fast" (source also of Old Irish riag "torture," Middle High German ric "band, string").

meanings.definitionCn: 重复， 复现或复制； 再制造； 再生

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 折叠/交织 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "repeat," from Latin replicatus, past participle of replicare (see reply).

meanings.definitionCn: [电子] 芯片；筹码；碎片；(食物的) 小片; 薄片

etymology.story: early 15c., "to chip" (intransitive, of stone); from Old English forcippian "to pare away by cutting, cut off," verbal form of cipp "small piece of wood" (see chip (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 沉入/合并 + ance

etymology.story: 1640s, "unforeseen occurrence," from French émergence, from emerger, from Latin emergere "rise up" (see emerge).

"important arrival," 1742, an extended sense of  Advent "season before Christmas" (Old English), from Latin adventus "a coming, approach, arrival," in Church Latin "the coming of the Savior," from past participle stem of advenire "arrive, come to," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + venire "to come" (see venue).

meanings.definitionCn: 中世纪的,中古(时代)的,老式的,原始的

etymology.story: 1827, "pertaining to or suggestive of the Middle Ages," coined in English from Latin medium "the middle" (see medium (n.)) + aevum "age" (see eon)..

early 14c., transitive and intransitive, from Old French assembler "come together, join, unite; gather" (11c.), from Latin assimulare "to make like, liken, compare; copy, imitate; feign, pretend," later "to gather together," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + simulare "to make like" (see simulation).

1530s, "delude, make a fool of," from Latin eludere "finish play, win at play; escape from or parry (a blow), make a fool of, mock, frustrate; win from at play," from assimilated form of ex- "out, away" (see ex-) + ludere "to play" (see ludicrous).

meanings.definitionCn: 过渡；转变；[分子生物] 转换；变调

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin transitionem (nominative transitio) "a going across or over," noun of action from past participle stem of transire "go or cross over" (see transient)..
Old English blæstan "to blow, belch forth," from the root of blast (n.).

root.meaning: to be scorekeeper, to keep the score in a game or contest

morphology.mnemonic: 不足/在…之下 + to be scorekeeper, to keep the score in a game or contest

etymology.story: 1771, "to draw a line under," from under + score (v.).
late 13c., bilage "local ordinance," from Old Norse or Old Danish bi-lagu "town law," from byr "place where people dwell, town, village," from bua "to dwell" (see bower) + lagu "law" (see law).

mid-15c., Scottish, "postpone, defer," from Middle French superceder "desist, delay, defer," from Latin supersedere literally "sit on top of;" also, with ablative, "stay clear of, abstain from, forbear, refrain from," from super "above" (see super-) + sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

meanings.definitionCn: 耕作， 栽培； 养殖；培养， 教养； 磨炼

etymology.story: early 17c., from Medieval Latin cultivatus, past participle of cultivare "to cultivate," from Late Latin cultivus "tilled," from Latin cultus (see cult).

morphology.mnemonic: different in character or quality. + 使…化/造成 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 分析的；解析的；善于分析的

etymology.story: 1570s, "form of speech of a region or group," from Middle French dialecte, from Latin dialectus "local language, way of speaking, conversation," from Greek dialektos "talk, conversation, speech;" also "the language of a country, dialect," from dialegesthai "converse with each other," from dia- "across, between" (see dia-) + legein "speak" (see lecture (n.))..
1824; see archaeology + -ist..

late 14c., from Old French rigor "strength, hardness" (13c., Modern French rigueur), from Latin rigorem (nominative rigor) "numbness, stiffness, hardness, firmness; roughness, rudeness," from rigere "be stiff" (see rigid)..

root.meaning: to reckon for money given or received, render a reckoning,

morphology.mnemonic: to reckon for money given or received, render a reckoning, + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: mid-15c., "accounting officer, one who renders accounts," from Old French acuntant (Modern French accomptant), from present participle of accompter (see account (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 紧急情况,突然事件,非常时刻

etymology.story: "unforeseen occurrence requiring immediate attention," 1630s, from Latin emergens, present participle of emergere "to rise out or up" (see emerge).

meanings.definitionCn: 消遣的， 娱乐的； 游戏的

etymology.story: 1650s, from recreation + -al (1).
1780s, "to adopt for the stage," see drama (Greek stem dramat-) + -ize.

meanings.definitionCn: 膨胀； 凸出； 塞满
典范的；惩戒性的；可仿效的

morphology.mnemonic: that which is taken out, + 与…有关的/像…的 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1580s, "fit to be an example," from Middle French exemplaire, from Late Latin exemplaris "that serves as an example, pattern, or motto," from exemplum "example, pattern, model" (see example).

late 14c., "ability, opportunity, means, resources," from Old French faculte "skill, accomplishment, learning" (14c., Modern French faculté) and directly from Latin facultatem (nominative facultas) "power, ability, capability, opportunity; sufficient number, abundance, wealth," from *facli-tat-s, from facilis (see facile).
1550s, from Middle French absurde (16c.), from Latin absurdus "out of tune; foolish" (see absurdity).
late 14c., from lime (n.1) + stone (n.)..
early 14c., porpas, from Old French porpais (12c.) "porpoise," literally "pork fish," from porc "pork" (see pork (n.)) + peis "fish," from Latin piscis "fish" (see fish (n.)).

late 15c., "shot, missiles;" later "a stroke in drawing, a short line" (1580s), from Middle French trait "line, stroke, feature, tract," from Latin tractus "drawing, drawing out, dragging, pulling," later "line drawn, feature," from past participle stem of trahere "to pull, draw" (see tract (n.1)).
"rock that falls to earth, after streaking across the sky as a meteor," 1818, from meteor + -ite..
late 13c., from archaic past participle of Old English meltian, a class III strong verb (see melt (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 半径，半径范围；[解剖] 桡骨；辐射光线；有效航程

etymology.story: 1590s, "cross-shaft," from Latin radius "staff, stake, rod; spoke of a wheel; ray of light, beam of light; radius of a circle," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 破裂， 分裂； 瓦解

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + untouched, + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1796, noun of action from disintegrate..
1630s, "a setting free," from French émancipation, from Latin emancipationem (nominative emancipatio), noun of action from past participle stem of emancipare (see emancipate).

meanings.definitionCn: 献身的，一心一意的，热诚的

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 再/重新/回 + quire

etymology.story: 1630s (n.) "something required beforehand," 1650s (adj.), "required beforehand," both from pre- + requisite..

meanings.definitionCn: 排出；耗尽；使精疲力尽；彻底探讨

etymology.story: "waste gas," 1848, originally from steam engines, from exhaust (v.).
Old English þær æfter; see there + after.
mid-15c., "the proving of guilt," from Late Latin convictionem (nominative convictio) "proof, refutation," noun of action from past participle stem of convincere (see convince).

morphology.mnemonic: different in character or quality. + 使…化/造成

etymology.story: late 15c., from Old French diversifier (13c.) "to make diverse," from Medieval Latin diversificare, from Latin diversus (see diverse).
1878, shortening of graphic formula (see graphic).

root.meaning: stand in some relation; have reference or respect

morphology.mnemonic: stand in some relation; have reference or respect + 有…倾向的 + ity

etymology.story: 1834, "fact or condition of being relative" (apparently coined by Coleridge, of God, in "Notes on Waterland's Vindication of Christ's Divinity"), from relative (adj.) + -ity.

mid-15c., "implant a bud into a plant," from Latin inoculatus, past participle of inoculare "graft in, implant a bud or eye of one plant into another," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + oculus "bud," originally "eye" (see eye (n.)).
1630s, from Latin excerptum "an extract, selection," noun use of neuter past participle of excerpere "to extract" (see excerpt (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: hexa + gon + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1570s, from hexagon + -al (1).

1743, borrowed (during an outbreak of the disease in Europe), from Italian influenza "influenza, epidemic," originally "visitation, influence (of the stars)," from Medieval Latin influentia in the astrological sense (see influence).

meanings.definitionCn: 引起分歧的， 导致分裂的

morphology.mnemonic: watershed, separation between river valleys + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1727, from Latin auditorium "lecture room," literally "place where something is heard," noun use of neuter of auditorius (adj.) "of or for hearing," from auditus, past participle of audire "to hear" (see audience); also see -ory.

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story:
1520s, from Medieval Latin analyticus 
(see analytic) + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 个人主义, 利己主义

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + watershed, separation between river valleys + 与…有关的/形容词 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: "quality of being distinct or individual, individuality," 1815, from individual + -ism.

meanings.definitionCn: 触觉的； 有触觉的； 可感触的

etymology.story: 1610s, "perceptible to touch," from French tactile (16c.) and directly from Latin tactilis "tangible, that may be touched," from tactus, past participle of tangere "to touch" (see tangent (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 会议；（法庭的）开庭；（议会等的）开会；学期；讲习会

etymology.story: late 14c., "periodical sitting of a court," from Old French session "act or state of sitting; assembly," from Latin sessionem (nominative sessio) "act of sitting; a seat; loitering; a session," noun of action from past participle stem of sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

morphology.mnemonic: intra + 引导 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1550s, a contraction of a pece (mid-15c.), originally of coins, objects for sale, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 期间；持久， 持续时间

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French duration, from Medieval Latin durationem (nominative duratio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin durare "harden" (see endure).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin accretionem (nominative accretio) "an increasing, a growing larger" (as of the waxing moon), noun of action from past participle stem of accrescere, from ad- "to" (see ad-) + crescere "grow" (see crescent)..

meanings.definitionCn: 适用于种植园或热带、亚热带国家的

morphology.mnemonic: to bury + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., "action of planting," from Middle French plantation, from Latin plantationem (nominative plantatio) "a planting," noun of action from past participle stem of plantare "to plant" (see plant).

meanings.definitionCn: 自我中心的， 自私自利的

morphology.mnemonic: n. 自我，自己 + …主义者/…专家 + ic

etymology.story: 1530s, "to give (legal) confirmation to," from Latin corroboratus, past participle of corroborare "to strengthen, invigorate," from com- "together" or "thoroughly" (see com-) + roborare "to make strong," from  robur, robus "strength," (see robust).
"chemical that kills plants," used to destroy unwanted weeds, etc., 1888, originally a trademark name, from herb + -cide..

late 14c., "action of making a contract" (especially of marriage), also "action of shrinking or shortening," from Old French contraction (13c.), or directly from Latin contractionem (nominative contractio), noun of action from past participle stem of contrahere (see contract (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使…变得淡而无味；除掉…的特性
变得平淡无奇；丧失特性

etymology.story: 1660s, from Italian blando "delicate," or Old French bland "flattering, complimentary," both from Latin blandus "smooth-talking, flattering, alluring," perhaps from PIE *mlad-, nasalized variant of *meld-, extended form of root *mel- (see melt).

meanings.definitionCn: 游荡；游手好闲；虚度光阴

etymology.story: 1835, American English, back-formation from loafer (1830).
popular name for a bacterium, 1878, from French microbe, "badly coined ...

meanings.definitionCn: 接待,招待会;接收,接受,接收效果

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 取/抓 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., in astrology, "effect of two planets on each other;" sense of "act of receiving" is recorded from late 15c., from Latin receptionem (nominative receptio) "a receiving," noun of action from past participle stem of recipere (see receive).

meanings.definitionCn: 原子能的；[细胞] 细胞核的；中心的；原子核的

etymology.story: 1841, "of or like the nucleus of a cell," from nucleus + -ar, probably by influence of French nucléaire.
also aero-dynamic, 1847; see aero- + dynamic (adj.).
"occupy oneself in a trifling way," 1740, earlier "to poke again and again" (1520s), frequentative of obsolete verb poten "to push, poke," from Old English potian "to push" (see put (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 月亮的，月球的；阴历的；银的；微亮的

morphology.mnemonic: moon月亮 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: "crescent-shaped," early 15c.; "pertaining to the moon," 1620s, from Old French lunaire (15c.), from Latin lunaris "of the moon," from luna "moon" (with capital L- "moon goddess"); see Luna..

meanings.definitionCn: 伐木；喧闹地向前走；笨重地行动，缓慢地移动
木材；废物，无用的杂物；隆隆声
[电] 继电器；接替，接替人员；驿马

etymology.story: Old English preost probably shortened from the older Germanic form represented by Old Saxon and Old High German prestar, Old Frisian prestere, all from Vulgar Latin *prester "priest," from Late Latin presbyter "presbyter, elder," from Greek presbyteros (see Presbyterian).

early 15c., "protection, care, custody," from Anglo-French tuycioun (13c.), Old French tuicion "guardianship," from Latin tuitionem (nominative tuitio) "a looking after, a caring for, watching over, protection, guardianship," from tuitus, past participle of tueri "to look after" (see tutor (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 反射的, 反省的, 反作用的, 优角的

etymology.story: 1650s, from Latin permeatus, past participle of permeare "to pass through" (see permeable).

meanings.definitionCn: 可触知的；明确的；确实的

etymology.story: 1580s, "capable of being touched," from Middle French tangible and directly from Late Latin tangibilis "that may be touched," from Latin tangere "to touch" (see tangent (adj.)).
"chronic inability to sleep," 1620s, insomnie, from Latin insomnia "want of sleep, sleeplessness," from insomnis "sleepless," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + somnus "sleep" (see Somnus).
1580s, "sending of mail by post;" 1650s as "cost of sending something by mail," from post (n.3) + -age.
early 15c., from medical Latin intestinalis, from Latin intestinum "an intestine, gut" (see intestine)..
1650s, possibly a native formation, or else from French détérioration (15c.), noun of action from détériorer, from Late Latin deteriorare (see deteriorate)..

meanings.definitionCn: 派别；内讧；小集团；纪实小说

morphology.mnemonic: 事实 + 状态/行为/过程
看/观察 + acul + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1680s, from Latin spectaculum "a sight, show" (see spectacle) + -ar.

meanings.definitionCn: 特殊的；独特的；奇怪的；罕见的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "belonging exclusively to one person," from Latin peculiaris "of one's own (property)," from peculium "private property," literally "property in cattle" (in ancient times the most important form of property), from pecu "cattle, flock," related to pecus "cattle" (see pecuniary).
1620s, from French recouper "to cut back" (12c.), from Old French re- "back" (see re-) + couper "to cut," from coup "a blow" (see coup).

in early use also graunt, early 13c., "to allow, permit (something); consent to (a prayer, request, etc.)," from Old French graanter, variant of creanter "assure, promise, guarantee, swear; confirm, authorize, approve (of)," from Latin credentem (nominative credens), present participle of credere "to believe, to trust" (see credo).

meanings.definitionCn: 注射， 注入； 充满

morphology.mnemonic: in + 投/掷 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "a forcing of a fluid into a body" (with a syringe, etc.), early 15c., from Old French iniection (14c.) or directly from Latin iniectionem (nominative iniectio) "a throwing in," noun of action from past participle stem of inicere "to throw in or on" (see inject)..

meanings.definitionCn: 主要产品；订书钉；主题；主食
主要的，大宗生产的；常用的；纺织纤维的

morphology.mnemonic: cylind + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1640s, probably from cylindric (but this is attested only from 1680s), from Greek kylindrikos, from kylindros (see cylinder) + -al (1)..

early 13c., "body part or other object from a holy person," from Old French relique (11c., plural reliques), from Late Latin reliquiæ (plural) "remains of a martyr," in classical Latin "remains, remnants," noun use of fem.
1836, from paleontology + -ist..

1620s, "reckon as an abatement or deduction," from Old French desconter (13c., Modern French décompter), from Medieval Latin discomputare, from dis- (see dis-) + computare "to count" (see count (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 变换,转换；改变（信仰等）；兑换（钱）

morphology.mnemonic: roy + 与…有关的/形容词 + ity
共同/联合 + 劳动/工作 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1871, back-formation from collaborator.

morphology.mnemonic: a flowing, flow + esc + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1853 (Stokes), from fluor- (see fluoro-) + -escent (see fluorescence).

Old English seonowe, oblique form of nominative sionu "sinew," from Proto-Germanic *senawo (source also of Old Saxon sinewa, Old Norse sina, Old Frisian sine, Middle Dutch senuwe, Dutch zenuw, Old High German senawa, German Sehne), from PIE root *sai- "to tie, bind" (source also of Sanskrit snavah "sinew," Avestan snavar, Irish sin "chain")..

meanings.definitionCn: 分,分割;部门,科,处;除法;分界线

morphology.mnemonic: watershed, separation between river valleys + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French division, from Latin divisionem (nominative divisio), from divid-, stem of dividere (see divide).

morphology.mnemonic: 超/在…之上 + 做/制造 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., in anatomical and mathematical uses, "of or relating to a surface," from Late Latin superficialis "of or pertaining to the surface," from superficies "surface, upper side, top," from super "above, over" (see super-) + facies "form, face" (see face (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 总经理,董事,行政负责人

morphology.mnemonic: to inflict capital punishment + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "performed, carried out;" 1640s, "of the branch of government that carries out the laws," from Middle French executif, from Latin executivus, from past participle stem of exequi "follow after; carry out, accomplish" (see execution).

root.meaning: a nation's collective, organized sea power

morphology.mnemonic: a nation's collective, organized sea power + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1530s, from Middle French navigation (14c.) or directly from Latin navigationem (nominative navigatio) "a sailing, navigation, voyage," noun of action from past participle stem of navigare "to sail, sail over, go by sea, steer a ship," from navis "ship" (see naval) + root of agere "to drive" (see act (n.))..

root.meaning: sharp-pointed organ capable of inflicting a painful puncture wound

morphology.mnemonic: sharp-pointed organ capable of inflicting a painful puncture wound + er

etymology.story: 1550s, agent noun from sting (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 意外事故；伤亡人员；急诊室

etymology.story: early 15c., "chance, accident; incidental charge," from casual (adj.) on model of royalty, penalty, etc.

root.meaning: having to do with outer space

morphology.mnemonic: having to do with outer space + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1840 (spacial is from 1838), "occupying space," from Latin spatium + adjectival suffix -al (1); formed in English as an adjective to space (n.), to go with temporal.

morphology.mnemonic: in + 看/观察 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French inspeccion "inspection, examination" (13c., Modern French inspection), from Latin inspectionem (nominative inspectio) "a looking into," noun of action from past participle stem of inspicere "look at, observe, view; look into, inspect, examine," from in- "into" (see in- (2)) + specere "to look" (see scope (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 计量器；标准尺寸；容量规格

etymology.story: early 15c., "fixed standard of measure" (surname Gageman is early 14c.), from Old North French gauge "gauging rod" (see gauge (v.)).
"to blend together, merge, unite" (intransitive), by 1910, of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 好学的， 学习勤勉的； 专心的； 谨慎的， 认真的， 仔细的

morphology.mnemonic: stud + 具有…特征/名词化 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: 1670s, "absurd thing;" 1680s, "words or actions meant to invoke ridicule," from French ridicule, noun use of adjective (15c.), or from Latin ridiculum "laughing matter, joke," from noun use of neuter of ridiculus (see ridiculous)..

root.meaning: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning

morphology.mnemonic: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning  + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., in reference to the body or its parts, "having the function of sensation;" also (early 15c.) "pertaining to the faculty of the soul that receives and analyzes sensory information;" from Old French sensitif "capable of feeling" (13c.) and directly from Medieval Latin sensitivus "capable of sensation," from Latin sensus, past participle of sentire "feel perceive" (see sense (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 酿造； 冲泡； 酝酿
空的,未占用的;空缺的;神情茫然的
揭穿；拆穿…的假面具；暴露

etymology.story: 1923,  from de- + bunk (n.2); first used by U.S.

early 15c., "a lobe of the liver or lungs," from Middle French lobe and directly from Medieval Latin lobus, from Late Latin lobus "hull, husk, pod," from Greek lobos "lobe of the ear, vegetable pod," perhaps related to Greek leberis "husk of fruits," from PIE *logwos.
1838, from Latin iratus "angry, enraged, violent, furious," past participle of irasci "grow angry," from ira "anger" (see ire)..

meanings.definitionCn: 有道德的， 品性好的， 品德高的； 自命清高的； 贞洁的

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 弯曲

etymology.story: 1550s, from Latin deflectere "to bend (something) aside or downward," from de- "away" (see de-) + flectere "to bend" (see flexible).

meanings.definitionCn: 间隔,间歇;(幕间或工间)休息

etymology.story: early 14c., "time elapsed between two actions or events," from Old French intervalle "interval, interim" (14c.), earlier entreval (13c.) and directly from Late Latin intervallum "a space between, an interval of time, a distance," originally "space between palisades or ramparts" [OED], from inter "between" (see inter-) + vallum "rampart, palisade, wall," which is apparently a collective form of vallus "stake," from PIE *walso- "a post" (see wall (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 合法的；合理的，合乎逻辑的

etymology.story: "establish the legitimacy of, make lawful," 1590s, from Medieval Latin legitimatus, past participle of legitimare "make lawful" (see legitimate (adj.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 强壮/价值 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1831, back-formation from evaluation, or else from French évaluer, back-formation from évaluation.

meanings.definitionCn: 地位；状态；情形；重要身份

etymology.story: 1670s, "height" of a situation or condition, later "legal standing of a person" (1791), from Latin status "condition, position, state, manner, attitude" from past participle stem of stare "to stand," from PIE *ste-tu-, from root *stā- "to stand" (see stet).
Old English gescildan, from the root of shield (n.).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: late 14c., "study of the structure of living beings;" c.
"perplexed, confused," 1680s, past participle adjective from embarrass..

meanings.definitionCn: 外来的；没有关联的；来自体外的

etymology.story: 1630s, from Latin extraneus "external, strange," literally "that is without, from without" (as a noun, "a stranger"), from extra "outside of" (see extra-).

meanings.definitionCn: 交付， 递送； 释放；发表； 表达；接生

etymology.story: 1650s, from instant (n.) on model of spontaneous, etc.

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + semin + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1570s, "fact, occurrence," from Late Latin phænomenon, from Greek phainomenon "that which appears or is seen," noun use of neuter present participle of phainesthai "to appear," passive of phainein (see phantasm).

morphology.mnemonic: notice, attention, reputation + 使…化/造成

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French notefiier "make known, inform, apprise" (13c.), from Latin notificare "to make known, notify," from Latin notus "known" (see notice (n.)) + root of facere "make, do" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 大批离去， 成群外出

etymology.story: late Old English, the second book of the Old Testament, from Latin exodus, from Greek exodos "a military expedition; a solemn procession; departure; death," literally "a going out," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + hodos "way" (see cede).
1827, from mal- "bad, badly, wrong" + function.

1610s, "worship," also "a particular form of worship," from French culte (17c.), from Latin cultus "care, labor; cultivation, culture; worship, reverence," originally "tended, cultivated," past participle of colere "to till" (see colony).

meanings.definitionCn: 轻快的；有浮力的；上涨的

morphology.mnemonic: rise up, lift, sustain + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1570s, perhaps from Spanish boyante, present participle of boyar "to float," from boya "buoy," from Dutch boei (see buoy (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: (尤指不好的事)一再发生；重现

etymology.story: late 14c., "recover from illness or suffering;" mid-15c., "to return" (to a place), from Latin recurrere "to return, run back, hasten back," figuratively "revert, recur," from re- "back, again" (see re-) + currere "to run" (see current (adj.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 堆积/积累 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin accumulatus, past participle of accumulare "to heap up" (see accumulation).
1530s, "pertaining to the earliest period, primitive, ancient," from Middle French pristin or directly from Latin pristinus "former, early, original," from Old Latin pri "before" (see prime (adj.)).

1610s, "the growing together of parts," from French coalition (1540s), from Late Latin coalitus "fellowship," originally past participle of Latin coalescere "unite, grow together, become one in growth" (see coalesce).

meanings.definitionCn: 肯定的, (对正式辩论中的问题)表示赞成的, (态度, 方法等)积极的, 乐观的, 怀有希望的

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 坚固/稳定 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: "answering 'yes,'" mid-15c., from use in logic; from Middle French affirmatif (13c.), from Latin affirmativus, from affirmat-, past participle stem of affirmare (see affirm).

meanings.definitionCn: 宣称，断言；提出…作为理由

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + regul + 与…有关的/像…的 + ity

etymology.story: early 14c., "violation of Church rules governing admission to clerical office," from Old French irregularité (14c.) and directly from Medieval Latin irregularitas "irregularity," from irregularis "not regular" (see irregular (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 扳机；[电子] 触发器；制滑机
肯定的,积极的,绝对的,无疑的,正的
耕种， 耕作； 培养， 教育

morphology.mnemonic: 耕，培养 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French computation, from Latin computationem (nominative computatio), noun of action from past participle stem of computare "to sum up, reckon, compute" (see compute)..

morphology.mnemonic: 肾 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-14c., "rent roll;" late 14c., "income from rents," from Anglo-French rental, Medieval Latin rentale; see rent (n.1) + -al (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 艺术品鉴赏家；古董收藏家；艺术大师
行家里手的；艺术爱好者的

etymology.story: 1610s, "scholar, connoisseur," from Italian virtuoso (plural virtuosi), noun use of adjective meaning "skilled, learned, of exceptional worth," from Late Latin virtuosus (see virtuous).

meanings.definitionCn: 难民，避难者；流亡者，逃亡者

etymology.story: 1680s, from French refugié, noun use of past participle of refugier "to take shelter, protect," from Old French refuge (see refuge).
"relating to muscular motion," 1841, from Greek kinetikos "moving, putting in motion," from kinetos "moved," verbal adjective of kinein "to move" (see cite).
early 15c., from Middle French comparable, from Latin comparabilis "capable of comparison," from comparare (see comparison).
1610s, "to indicate specially," from special (adj.) + -ize, perhaps on model of French spécialiser.

morphology.mnemonic: to dwell permanently + ance + 具有…特征/名词化
